src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

     1	// 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/api-key-stamper@0.5.0
     2	//
     3	// Upstream snapshot:
     4	//   codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-api-key-stamper-0.5.0/
     5	//
     6	// Files covered:
     7	//   ts-source/index.ts                -> ApiKeyStamper class + signWithApiKey
     8	//                                         + stampHeaderName constant
     9	//   ts-source/purejs.ts               -> signWithApiKey "purejs" runtime
    10	//                                         (P-256 ECDSA via @noble/curves,
    11	//                                          SHA-256, DER hex output)
    12	//
    13	// Runtime selection note:
    14	//   The upstream package supports three runtimes — "browser" (WebCrypto),
    15	//   "node" (Node crypto), "purejs" (@noble/curves). This C# port mirrors
    16	//   the **purejs** runtime since BouncyCastle exposes the same primitives
    17	//   in a single backend. There is no runtime detection in C#; behavior is
    18	//   identical to what upstream produces when `runtimeOverride = "purejs"`
    19	//   is passed.
    20	//
    21	// Wire bytes:
    22	//   - signature.toDERHex() output equals upstream's noble signature.toDERHex().
    23	//   - The stamp JSON shape is exactly the upstream's
    24	//     { publicKey, scheme, signature } in this order.
    25	//   - The X-Stamp header value is base64url(JSON), matching upstream's
    26	//     `stringToBase64urlString(JSON.stringify(stamp))`.
    27	//   - SHA-256(content) and deterministic ECDSA (RFC 6979) are used; low-S is
    28	//     enforced (noble defaults to lowS: true).
    29	
    30	using System;
    31	using System.IO;
    32	using Org.BouncyCastle.Asn1.X9;
    33	using Org.BouncyCastle.Crypto.Parameters;
    34	using Org.BouncyCastle.Crypto.Signers;
    35	using Org.BouncyCastle.Math;
    36	using Org.BouncyCastle.Security;
    37	
    38	namespace Turnkey
    39	{
    40	    /// <summary>
    41	    /// Stamper for Turnkey API requests. 1:1 logical port of upstream
    42	    /// <c>@turnkey/api-key-stamper</c> at peak's pinned version 0.5.0.
    43	    /// </summary>
    44	    /// <remarks>
    45	    /// <para><b>Runtime parity target:</b> this port mirrors upstream's
    46	    /// <c>purejs</c> runtime (deterministic ECDSA via RFC 6979 + low-S).
    47	    /// Upstream's default <c>detectRuntime()</c> picks <c>"node"</c> in
    48	    /// Node which dispatches to <c>nodecrypto.ts</c> and produces
    49	    /// non-deterministic signatures (random k); behavior parity with
    50	    /// that runtime is intentionally NOT a goal — Turnkey's backend
    51	    /// accepts either, but the C# wire bytes will only match
    52	    /// deterministically against upstream <c>runtimeOverride: "purejs"</c>
    53	    /// callers. All three runtimes produce signatures that verify against
    54	    /// the same public key, so end-to-end Turnkey API calls work
    55	    /// regardless.</para>
    56	    /// <para><b>Validation timing:</b> the constructor parses but does NOT
    57	    /// validate the private key. Validation happens in
    58	    /// <see cref="SignWithApiKey(string)"/>, matching upstream which only
    59	    /// constructs noble's key object at sign time.</para>
    60	    /// </remarks>
    61	    public class ApiKeyStamper
    62	    {
    63	        /// <summary>Header name used by Turnkey API requests.</summary>
    64	        public const string StampHeaderName = "X-Stamp";
    65	
    66	        /// <summary>Turnkey signature scheme identifier for P-256 API keys.</summary>
    67	        public const string SignatureScheme = "SIGNATURE_SCHEME_TK_API_P256";
    68	
    69	        private readonly string _apiPublicKey;
    70	        private readonly string _apiPrivateKey;
    71	
    72	        /// <summary>
    73	        /// Initialize the stamper with a P-256 API key pair.
    74	        /// </summary>
    75	        /// <param name="apiPublicKey">Compressed P-256 public key as hex (66 chars).</param>
    76	        /// <param name="apiPrivateKey">P-256 private key scalar as 64 hex chars.</param>
    77	        /// <remarks>
    78	        /// The constructor only stores the strings. Key parsing and range
    79	        /// validation happen lazily in <see cref="SignWithApiKey(string)"/>,
    80	        /// matching upstream which builds noble's key object on each sign.
    81	        /// </remarks>
    82	        public ApiKeyStamper(string apiPublicKey, string apiPrivateKey)
    83	        {
    84	            _apiPublicKey = apiPublicKey ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(apiPublicKey));
    85	            _apiPrivateKey = apiPrivateKey ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(apiPrivateKey));
    86	        }
    87	
    88	        /// <summary>
    89	        /// Output of <see cref="Stamp(string)"/>: the header name and the
    90	        /// base64url-encoded stamp JSON to use as the header value.
    91	        /// </summary>
    92	        public class StampResult
    93	        {
    94	            public string StampHeaderName { get; set; } = string.Empty;
    95	            public string StampHeaderValue { get; set; } = string.Empty;
    96	        }
    97	
    98	        /// <summary>
    99	        /// Internal JSON shape of the stamp. Field order matches upstream
   100	        /// (<c>publicKey, scheme, signature</c>) so the base64url-encoded
   101	        /// header value is wire-identical to what
   102	        /// <c>JSON.stringify({ publicKey, scheme, signature })</c> produces.
   103	        /// </summary>
   104	        public class TurnkeyStamp
   105	        {
   106	            [System.Text.Json.Serialization.JsonPropertyName("publicKey")]
   107	            public string PublicKey { get; set; } = string.Empty;
   108	
   109	            [System.Text.Json.Serialization.JsonPropertyName("scheme")]
   110	            public string Scheme { get; set; } = string.Empty;
   111	
   112	            [System.Text.Json.Serialization.JsonPropertyName("signature")]
   113	            public string Signature { get; set; } = string.Empty;
   114	        }
   115	
   116	        /// <summary>
   117	        /// Produce a Turnkey API stamp for <paramref name="payload"/>.
   118	        /// Equivalent to upstream <c>ApiKeyStamper.stamp(payload)</c>.
   119	        /// </summary>
   120	        public StampResult Stamp(string payload)
   121	        {
   122	            if (payload == null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(payload));
   123	
   124	            string signatureDerHex = SignWithApiKey(payload);
   125	
   126	            var stamp = new TurnkeyStamp
   127	            {
   128	                PublicKey = _apiPublicKey,
   129	                Scheme = SignatureScheme,
   130	                Signature = signatureDerHex,
   131	            };
   132	
   133	            string stampJson = System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer.Serialize(
   134	                stamp, TurnkeyJsonContext.Default.TurnkeyStamp);
   135	
   136	            return new StampResult
   137	            {
   138	                StampHeaderName = StampHeaderName,
   139	                StampHeaderValue = Encoding.StringToBase64UrlString(stampJson),
   140	            };
   141	        }
   142	
   143	        /// <summary>
   144	        /// Mirror of upstream <c>purejs.ts signWithApiKey</c>. Verifies that
   145	        /// the configured public key matches the one derived from the
   146	        /// configured private key, then SHA-256-hashes the payload and signs
   147	        /// it with deterministic ECDSA (RFC 6979 + low-S) returning the
   148	        /// DER-encoded signature as a hex string.
   149	        /// </summary>
   150	        public string SignWithApiKey(string content)
   151	        {
   152	            if (content == null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(content));
   153	
   154	            // Upstream `purejs.ts`:
   155	            //   const publicKey = p256.getPublicKey(input.privateKey, true);
   156	            //   const publicKeyString = uint8ArrayToHexString(publicKey);
   157	            //   if (publicKeyString != input.publicKey) throw new Error(...);
   158	            //
   159	            // Crypto.GetPublicKey enforces the noble invariants (exact 32-byte
   160	            // private key, scalar in [1, n - 1]) before deriving the public
   161	            // key. Per the upstream pattern this validation happens at sign
   162	            // time, not at constructor time.
   163	            byte[] derivedPublicKeyBytes = Crypto.GetPublicKey(_apiPrivateKey, isCompressed: true);
   164	            string derivedPublicKey = Encoding.Uint8ArrayToHexString(derivedPublicKeyBytes);
   165	            if (!string.Equals(derivedPublicKey, _apiPublicKey, StringComparison.Ordinal))
   166	            {
   167	                throw new InvalidOperationException(
   168	                    "Bad API key. Expected to get public key " + _apiPublicKey
   169	                    + ", got " + derivedPublicKey);
   170	            }
   171	
   172	            // Upstream:
   173	            //   const hash = createHash().update(input.content).digest();   // SHA-256
   174	            //   const signature = p256.sign(hash, input.privateKey);        // RFC 6979 + lowS
   175	            //   return signature.toDERHex();
   176	            var curve = ECNamedCurveTable.GetByName(CryptoConstants.CURVE_NAME);
   177	            var domainParams = new ECDomainParameters(curve.Curve, curve.G, curve.N, curve.H, curve.GetSeed());
   178	            var privateKeyBytes = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(_apiPrivateKey);
   179	            var privateKeyScalar = new BigInteger(1, privateKeyBytes);
   180	
   181	            byte[] payloadBytes = System.Text.Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(content);
   182	
   183	            var digest = DigestUtilities.GetDigest("SHA-256");
   184	            digest.BlockUpdate(payloadBytes, 0, payloadBytes.Length);
   185	            var hash = new byte[digest.GetDigestSize()];
   186	            digest.DoFinal(hash, 0);
   187	
   188	            var hmacCalculator = new HMacDsaKCalculator(DigestUtilities.GetDigest("SHA-256"));
   189	            var signer = new ECDsaSigner(hmacCalculator);
   190	            signer.Init(true, new ECPrivateKeyParameters(privateKeyScalar, domainParams));
   191	
   192	            BigInteger[] signature = signer.GenerateSignature(hash);
   193	            BigInteger r = signature[0];
   194	            BigInteger s = signature[1];
   195	
   196	            // Low-S normalization (noble enables lowS by default).
   197	            BigInteger halfN = domainParams.N.ShiftRight(1);
   198	            if (s.CompareTo(halfN) > 0)
   199	            {
   200	                s = domainParams.N.Subtract(s);
   201	            }
   202	
   203	            return EncodeDerSignatureHex(r, s);
   204	        }
   205	
   206	        /// <summary>
   207	        /// Encode the <c>(r, s)</c> pair as a DER ASN.1 SEQUENCE of two
   208	        /// INTEGERs, then hex-encode the result.
   209	        /// Equivalent to noble's <c>signature.toDERHex()</c>.
   210	        /// </summary>
   211	        private static string EncodeDerSignatureHex(BigInteger r, BigInteger s)
   212	        {
   213	            byte[] rBytes = r.ToByteArrayUnsigned();
   214	            byte[] sBytes = s.ToByteArrayUnsigned();
   215	
   216	            bool rNeedsPadding = rBytes.Length > 0 && (rBytes[0] & 0x80) != 0;
   217	            bool sNeedsPadding = sBytes.Length > 0 && (sBytes[0] & 0x80) != 0;
   218	            int rLen = rBytes.Length + (rNeedsPadding ? 1 : 0);
   219	            int sLen = sBytes.Length + (sNeedsPadding ? 1 : 0);
   220	
   221	            int contentLen = 2 + rLen + 2 + sLen; // (INTEGER tag + length) * 2 + r + s
   222	
   223	            using (var ms = new MemoryStream())
   224	            {
   225	                ms.WriteByte(0x30); // SEQUENCE
   226	
   227	                if (contentLen >= 128)
   228	                {
   229	                    // Long-form length (1 octet count). For ECDSA P-256
   230	                    // signatures the total length never exceeds 0x7f in
   231	                    // practice; this branch is defensive.
   232	                    ms.WriteByte(0x81);
   233	                    ms.WriteByte((byte)contentLen);
   234	                }
   235	                else
   236	                {
   237	                    ms.WriteByte((byte)contentLen);
   238	                }
   239	
   240	                ms.WriteByte(0x02); // INTEGER tag (r)
   241	                ms.WriteByte((byte)rLen);
   242	                if (rNeedsPadding) ms.WriteByte(0x00);
   243	                ms.Write(rBytes, 0, rBytes.Length);
   244	
   245	                ms.WriteByte(0x02); // INTEGER tag (s)
   246	                ms.WriteByte((byte)sLen);
   247	                if (sNeedsPadding) ms.WriteByte(0x00);
   248	                ms.Write(sBytes, 0, sBytes.Length);
   249	
   250	                return Encoding.Uint8ArrayToHexString(ms.ToArray());
   251	            }
   252	        }
   253	    }
   254	}

     1	/// <reference lib="dom" />
     2	import { stringToBase64urlString } from "@turnkey/encoding";
     3	
     4	// Header name for an API key stamp
     5	const stampHeaderName = "X-Stamp";
     6	
     7	export type Runtime = "browser" | "node" | "purejs";
     8	
     9	export type TApiKeyStamperConfig = {
    10	  apiPublicKey: string;
    11	  apiPrivateKey: string;
    12	  runtimeOverride?: Runtime | undefined;
    13	};
    14	
    15	// `window.document` ensures that we're in a browser context
    16	// and `crypto.subtle` ensures that it supports the web crypto APIs
    17	// Inspired by https://github.com/flexdinesh/browser-or-node/blob/master/src/index.ts
    18	const isCryptoEnabledBrowser: boolean =
    19	  typeof window !== "undefined" &&
    20	  typeof window.document !== "undefined" &&
    21	  typeof crypto !== "undefined" &&
    22	  typeof crypto.subtle !== "undefined";
    23	
    24	// We check `process.versions.node`
    25	// Taken from https://github.com/flexdinesh/browser-or-node/blob/master/src/index.ts
    26	const isNode: boolean =
    27	  typeof process !== "undefined" &&
    28	  process.versions != null &&
    29	  process.versions.node != null;
    30	
    31	const detectRuntime = (): Runtime => {
    32	  if (isCryptoEnabledBrowser) {
    33	    return "browser";
    34	  }
    35	
    36	  if (isNode) {
    37	    return "node";
    38	  }
    39	
    40	  // If we don't have NodeJS or web crypto at our disposal, default to pure JS implementation
    41	  // This is the case for old browsers and react native environments
    42	  return "purejs";
    43	};
    44	
    45	/**
    46	 * Signature function abstracting the differences between NodeJS and web environments for signing with API keys.
    47	 */
    48	export const signWithApiKey = async (
    49	  input: {
    50	    content: string;
    51	    publicKey: string;
    52	    privateKey: string;
    53	  },
    54	  runtimeOverride?: Runtime | undefined,
    55	): Promise<string> => {
    56	  const runtime = runtimeOverride ?? detectRuntime();
    57	
    58	  switch (runtime) {
    59	    case "browser":
    60	      return (await import("./webcrypto")).signWithApiKey(input);
    61	    case "node":
    62	      return (await import("./nodecrypto")).signWithApiKey(input);
    63	    case "purejs":
    64	      return (await import("./purejs")).signWithApiKey(input);
    65	    default:
    66	      throw new Error(`Unsupported runtime: ${runtime}`);
    67	  }
    68	};
    69	
    70	/**
    71	 * Stamper to use with `@turnkey/http`'s `TurnkeyClient`
    72	 */
    73	export class ApiKeyStamper {
    74	  apiPublicKey: string;
    75	  apiPrivateKey: string;
    76	  runtimeOverride?: Runtime | undefined;
    77	
    78	  constructor(config: TApiKeyStamperConfig) {
    79	    this.apiPublicKey = config.apiPublicKey;
    80	    this.apiPrivateKey = config.apiPrivateKey;
    81	    this.runtimeOverride = config.runtimeOverride;
    82	  }
    83	
    84	  async stamp(payload: string) {
    85	    const signature = await signWithApiKey(
    86	      {
    87	        publicKey: this.apiPublicKey,
    88	        privateKey: this.apiPrivateKey,
    89	        content: payload,
    90	      },
    91	      this.runtimeOverride,
    92	    );
    93	
    94	    const stamp = {
    95	      publicKey: this.apiPublicKey,
    96	      scheme: "SIGNATURE_SCHEME_TK_API_P256",
    97	      signature,
    98	    };
    99	
   100	    return {
   101	      stampHeaderName,
   102	      stampHeaderValue: stringToBase64urlString(JSON.stringify(stamp)),
   103	    };
   104	  }
   105	}
   106	
   107	export { pointDecode } from "./tink/elliptic_curves";
     1	import { p256 } from "@noble/curves/p256";
     2	import { createHash } from "sha256-uint8array";
     3	import { uint8ArrayToHexString } from "@turnkey/encoding";
     4	
     5	export const signWithApiKey = async (input: {
     6	  content: string;
     7	  publicKey: string;
     8	  privateKey: string;
     9	}): Promise<string> => {
    10	  const publicKey = p256.getPublicKey(input.privateKey, true);
    11	
    12	  // Public key in the usual 02 or 03 + 64 hex digits
    13	  const publicKeyString = uint8ArrayToHexString(publicKey);
    14	
    15	  if (publicKeyString != input.publicKey) {
    16	    throw new Error(
    17	      `Bad API key. Expected to get public key ${input.publicKey}, got ${publicKeyString}`,
    18	    );
    19	  }
    20	
    21	  const hash = createHash().update(input.content).digest();
    22	  const signature = p256.sign(hash, input.privateKey);
    23	  return signature.toDERHex();
    24	};
     1	import { pointDecode } from "./tink/elliptic_curves";
     2	import {
     3	  hexStringToBase64url,
     4	  uint8ArrayFromHexString,
     5	  DEFAULT_JWK_MEMBER_BYTE_LENGTH,
     6	} from "@turnkey/encoding";
     7	
     8	/**
     9	 * Converts a Turnkey API key pair into a JSON Web Key (JWK) format.
    10	 * This function accepts P-256 API keys only.
    11	 *
    12	 * @param {Object} input - The Turnkey API key components.
    13	 * @param {string} input.uncompressedPrivateKeyHex - Hexadecimal-encoded uncompressed private key (32-byte scalar).
    14	 * @param {string} input.compressedPublicKeyHex - Hexadecimal-encoded compressed public key (33 bytes).
    15	 * @returns {JsonWebKey} A JSON Web Key object representing the EC P-256 key.
    16	 */
    17	export function convertTurnkeyApiKeyToJwk(input: {
    18	  uncompressedPrivateKeyHex: string;
    19	  compressedPublicKeyHex: string;
    20	}): JsonWebKey {
    21	  const { uncompressedPrivateKeyHex, compressedPublicKeyHex } = input;
    22	
    23	  let jwk;
    24	  try {
    25	    jwk = pointDecode(uint8ArrayFromHexString(compressedPublicKeyHex));
    26	  } catch (e) {
    27	    throw new Error(
    28	      `unable to load API key: invalid public key. Did you switch your public and private key by accident? Is your public key a valid, compressed P-256 public key?`,
    29	    );
    30	  }
    31	
    32	  // Ensure that d is sufficiently padded
    33	  jwk.d = hexStringToBase64url(
    34	    uncompressedPrivateKeyHex,
    35	    DEFAULT_JWK_MEMBER_BYTE_LENGTH,
    36	  );
    37	
    38	  return jwk;
    39	}
     1	import * as crypto from "crypto";
     2	import { convertTurnkeyApiKeyToJwk } from "./utils";
     3	
     4	export const signWithApiKey = async (input: {
     5	  content: string;
     6	  publicKey: string;
     7	  privateKey: string;
     8	}): Promise<string> => {
     9	  const { content, publicKey, privateKey } = input;
    10	
    11	  const privateKeyObject = crypto.createPrivateKey({
    12	    // @ts-expect-error -- the key can be a JWK object since Node v15.12.0
    13	    // https://nodejs.org/api/crypto.html#cryptocreateprivatekeykey
    14	    key: convertTurnkeyApiKeyToJwk({
    15	      uncompressedPrivateKeyHex: privateKey,
    16	      compressedPublicKeyHex: publicKey,
    17	    }),
    18	    format: "jwk",
    19	  });
    20	
    21	  const sign = crypto.createSign("SHA256");
    22	  sign.write(Buffer.from(content));
    23	  sign.end();
    24	
    25	  return sign.sign(privateKeyObject, "hex");
    26	};
     1	/// <reference lib="dom" />
     2	import { convertTurnkeyApiKeyToJwk } from "./utils";
     3	import { uint8ArrayToHexString } from "@turnkey/encoding";
     4	
     5	export const signWithApiKey = async (input: {
     6	  content: string;
     7	  publicKey: string;
     8	  privateKey: string;
     9	}): Promise<string> => {
    10	  const { content, publicKey, privateKey } = input;
    11	
    12	  const key = await importTurnkeyApiKey({
    13	    uncompressedPrivateKeyHex: privateKey,
    14	    compressedPublicKeyHex: publicKey,
    15	  });
    16	  return await signMessage({ key, content });
    17	};
    18	
    19	/**
    20	 * Imports a P-256 Turnkey API key into a WebCrypto `CryptoKey`.
    21	 *
    22	 * @param {Object} input - The Turnkey API key components.
    23	 * @param {string} input.uncompressedPrivateKeyHex - Hexadecimal-encoded uncompressed private key (32-byte scalar).
    24	 * @param {string} input.compressedPublicKeyHex - Hexadecimal-encoded compressed public key (33 bytes).
    25	 * @returns {Promise<CryptoKey>} A `CryptoKey` object representing a P-256 key.
    26	 */
    27	async function importTurnkeyApiKey(input: {
    28	  uncompressedPrivateKeyHex: string;
    29	  compressedPublicKeyHex: string;
    30	}): Promise<CryptoKey> {
    31	  const { uncompressedPrivateKeyHex, compressedPublicKeyHex } = input;
    32	
    33	  const jwk = convertTurnkeyApiKeyToJwk({
    34	    uncompressedPrivateKeyHex,
    35	    compressedPublicKeyHex,
    36	  });
    37	
    38	  return await crypto.subtle.importKey(
    39	    "jwk",
    40	    jwk,
    41	    {
    42	      name: "ECDSA",
    43	      namedCurve: "P-256",
    44	    },
    45	    false, // not extractable
    46	    ["sign"], // allow signing
    47	  );
    48	}
    49	
    50	async function signMessage(input: {
    51	  key: CryptoKey;
    52	  content: string;
    53	}): Promise<string> {
    54	  const { key, content } = input;
    55	
    56	  const signatureIeee1363 = await crypto.subtle.sign(
    57	    {
    58	      name: "ECDSA",
    59	      hash: "SHA-256",
    60	    },
    61	    key,
    62	    new TextEncoder().encode(content),
    63	  );
    64	
    65	  const signatureDer = convertEcdsaIeee1363ToDer(
    66	    new Uint8Array(signatureIeee1363),
    67	  );
    68	
    69	  return uint8ArrayToHexString(signatureDer);
    70	}
    71	
    72	/**
    73	 * `SubtleCrypto.sign(...)` outputs signature in IEEE P1363 format:
    74	 * - https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/SubtleCrypto/sign#ecdsa
    75	 *
    76	 * Turnkey expects the signature encoding to be DER-encoded ASN.1:
    77	 * - https://github.com/tkhq/tkcli/blob/7f0159af5a73387ff050647180d1db4d3a3aa033/src/internal/apikey/apikey.go#L149
    78	 *
    79	 * Code modified from https://github.com/google/tink/blob/6f74b99a2bfe6677e3670799116a57268fd067fa/javascript/subtle/elliptic_curves.ts#L114
    80	 *
    81	 * Transform an ECDSA signature in IEEE 1363 encoding to DER encoding.
    82	 *
    83	 * @param ieee the ECDSA signature in IEEE encoding
    84	 * @return ECDSA signature in DER encoding
    85	 */
    86	function convertEcdsaIeee1363ToDer(ieee: Uint8Array): Uint8Array {
    87	  if (ieee.length % 2 != 0 || ieee.length == 0 || ieee.length > 132) {
    88	    throw new Error(
    89	      "Invalid IEEE P1363 signature encoding. Length: " + ieee.length,
    90	    );
    91	  }
    92	  const r = toUnsignedBigNum(ieee.subarray(0, ieee.length / 2));
    93	  const s = toUnsignedBigNum(ieee.subarray(ieee.length / 2, ieee.length));
    94	  let offset = 0;
    95	  const length = 1 + 1 + r.length + 1 + 1 + s.length;
    96	  let der;
    97	  if (length >= 128) {
    98	    der = new Uint8Array(length + 3);
    99	    der[offset++] = 48;
   100	    der[offset++] = 128 + 1;
   101	    der[offset++] = length;
   102	  } else {
   103	    der = new Uint8Array(length + 2);
   104	    der[offset++] = 48;
   105	    der[offset++] = length;
   106	  }
   107	  der[offset++] = 2;
   108	  der[offset++] = r.length;
   109	  der.set(r, offset);
   110	  offset += r.length;
   111	  der[offset++] = 2;
   112	  der[offset++] = s.length;
   113	  der.set(s, offset);
   114	  return der;
   115	}
   116	
   117	/**
   118	 * Code modified from https://github.com/google/tink/blob/6f74b99a2bfe6677e3670799116a57268fd067fa/javascript/subtle/elliptic_curves.ts#L311
   119	 *
   120	 * Transform a big integer in big endian to minimal unsigned form which has
   121	 * no extra zero at the beginning except when the highest bit is set.
   122	 */
   123	function toUnsignedBigNum(bytes: Uint8Array): Uint8Array {
   124	  // Remove zero prefixes.
   125	  let start = 0;
   126	  while (start < bytes.length && bytes[start] == 0) {
   127	    start++;
   128	  }
   129	  if (start == bytes.length) {
   130	    start = bytes.length - 1;
   131	  }
   132	  let extraZero = 0;
   133	
   134	  // If the 1st bit is not zero, add 1 zero byte.
   135	  if ((bytes[start]! & 128) == 128) {
   136	    // Add extra zero.
   137	    extraZero = 1;
   138	  }
   139	  const res = new Uint8Array(bytes.length - start + extraZero);
   140	  res.set(bytes.subarray(start), extraZero);
   141	  return res;
   142	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// ApiKeyStamperTests.cs — xunit tests for src/ApiKeyStamper.cs.
     2	//
     3	// Vector sources:
     4	//   codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-api-key-stamper-0.5.0/ts-source/__fixtures__/api-key.{private,public}
     5	//   codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-api-key-stamper-0.5.0/ts-source/__tests__/stamp-test.ts
     6	//
     7	// P-256 ECDSA signatures are not deterministic across libraries (noble uses
     8	// RFC 6979; BouncyCastle's HMacDsaKCalculator also uses RFC 6979 with the
     9	// same digest, so for identical input + key + nonce-source the signature
    10	// is deterministic). Tests therefore assert:
    11	//   - the header name == "X-Stamp"
    12	//   - the base64url-decoded JSON has keys ["publicKey", "scheme", "signature"]
    13	//   - publicKey matches the configured key
    14	//   - scheme == "SIGNATURE_SCHEME_TK_API_P256"
    15	//   - the signature is a valid DER-encoded ECDSA signature over SHA-256(content)
    16	//     under the configured public key
    17	
    18	using System;
    19	using System.IO;
    20	using System.Text;
    21	using System.Text.Json;
    22	using FluentAssertions;
    23	using Org.BouncyCastle.Asn1;
    24	using Org.BouncyCastle.Asn1.X9;
    25	using Org.BouncyCastle.Crypto.Parameters;
    26	using Org.BouncyCastle.Math;
    27	using Org.BouncyCastle.Security;
    28	using Xunit;
    29	
    30	namespace Turnkey.Tests
    31	{
    32	    public class ApiKeyStamperTests
    33	    {
    34	        // From upstream __fixtures__/api-key.{private,public}
    35	        private const string FixturePrivateKey =
    36	            "487f361ddfd73440e707f4daa6775b376859e8a3c9f29b3bb694a12927c0213c";
    37	        private const string FixturePublicKey =
    38	            "02f739f8c77b32f4d5f13265861febd76e7a9c61a1140d296b8c16302508870316";
    39	
    40	        [Fact]
    41	        public void Constructor_DefersValidation()
    42	        {
    43	            // Upstream `new ApiKeyStamper({apiPublicKey, apiPrivateKey})` only
    44	            // assigns fields; validation happens at sign time. C# mirrors that.
    45	            Action act = () => new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, "abcd");
    46	            act.Should().NotThrow();
    47	        }
    48	
    49	        [Fact]
    50	        public void SignWithApiKey_RejectsBadKeyLength()
    51	        {
    52	            // Validation now occurs in SignWithApiKey -> Crypto.GetPublicKey.
    53	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, "abcd");
    54	            Action act = () => stamper.SignWithApiKey("payload");
    55	            act.Should().Throw<ArgumentException>()
    56	               .WithMessage("invalid P-256 private key: expected 32 bytes, got 2");
    57	        }
    58	
    59	        [Fact]
    60	        public void SignWithApiKey_RejectsZeroScalar()
    61	        {
    62	            string zero = new string('0', 64);
    63	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, zero);
    64	            Action act = () => stamper.SignWithApiKey("payload");
    65	            act.Should().Throw<ArgumentException>()
    66	               .WithMessage("invalid P-256 private key: scalar must be in [1, n - 1]");
    67	        }
    68	
    69	        [Fact]
    70	        public void Stamp_UpstreamFixture_ProducesValidWireBytes()
    71	        {
    72	            // Matches upstream __tests__/stamp-test.ts:6-37
    73	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
    74	            const string messageToSign = "hello from TKHQ!";
    75	
    76	            var result = stamper.Stamp(messageToSign);
    77	
    78	            result.StampHeaderName.Should().Be("X-Stamp");
    79	
    80	            // Decode base64url -> JSON -> object.
    81	            string decodedJson = Encoding.DecodeBase64UrlToString(result.StampHeaderValue);
    82	
    83	            using var doc = JsonDocument.Parse(decodedJson);
    84	            var root = doc.RootElement;
    85	
    86	            // Keys must be exactly publicKey, scheme, signature in this order.
    87	            int i = 0;
    88	            string[] expectedKeys = { "publicKey", "scheme", "signature" };
    89	            foreach (var prop in root.EnumerateObject())
    90	            {
    91	                prop.Name.Should().Be(expectedKeys[i++]);
    92	            }
    93	            i.Should().Be(3);
    94	
    95	            root.GetProperty("publicKey").GetString().Should().Be(FixturePublicKey);
    96	            root.GetProperty("scheme").GetString().Should().Be("SIGNATURE_SCHEME_TK_API_P256");
    97	
    98	            // Verify the DER ECDSA signature against SHA-256(content) under the
    99	            // public key. This is the wire-format gate: if the signature isn't
   100	            // verifiable against the configured P-256 key, the stamp would be
   101	            // rejected by Turnkey's backend.
   102	            string signatureHex = root.GetProperty("signature").GetString()!;
   103	            byte[] signatureBytes = HexToBytes(signatureHex);
   104	            AssertSignatureVerifies(FixturePublicKey, messageToSign, signatureBytes);
   105	        }
   106	
   107	        [Fact]
   108	        public void SignWithApiKey_ProducesDerHexThatVerifies()
   109	        {
   110	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
   111	            const string content = "any payload we care to sign";
   112	            string sigHex = stamper.SignWithApiKey(content);
   113	
   114	            // Output is DER hex; even length, starts with 30 (SEQUENCE tag).
   115	            (sigHex.Length % 2).Should().Be(0);
   116	            sigHex.Substring(0, 2).Should().Be("30");
   117	
   118	            AssertSignatureVerifies(FixturePublicKey, content, HexToBytes(sigHex));
   119	        }
   120	
   121	        [Fact]
   122	        public void SignWithApiKey_DeterministicWithRfc6979()
   123	        {
   124	            // RFC 6979 + same key + same content + same SHA-256(content) +
   125	            // same low-S normalization => signature is bit-identical across
   126	            // invocations (HMacDsaKCalculator is deterministic).
   127	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
   128	            string a = stamper.SignWithApiKey("the same content");
   129	            string b = stamper.SignWithApiKey("the same content");
   130	            a.Should().Be(b);
   131	        }
   132	
   133	        [Fact]
   134	        public void SignWithApiKey_LowSEnforced()
   135	        {
   136	            // For RFC 6979 + low-S: the s value's high bit (top byte) must be
   137	            // strictly less than (n / 2 + 1). Verify by parsing the DER and
   138	            // checking the s integer is in the lower half of [1, n-1].
   139	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
   140	            for (int i = 0; i < 8; i++)
   141	            {
   142	                string sigHex = stamper.SignWithApiKey("content_" + i);
   143	                var (r, s) = ParseDerEcdsa(HexToBytes(sigHex));
   144	
   145	                var curve = ECNamedCurveTable.GetByName(CryptoConstants.CURVE_NAME);
   146	                var halfN = curve.N.ShiftRight(1);
   147	                s.CompareTo(halfN).Should().BeLessOrEqualTo(0,
   148	                    "low-S requires s to be in (0, n/2]");
   149	                r.SignValue.Should().BeGreaterThan(0);
   150	                s.SignValue.Should().BeGreaterThan(0);
   151	            }
   152	        }
   153	
   154	        [Fact]
   155	        public void SignWithApiKey_WrongPublicKey_Throws()
   156	        {
   157	            // Mismatched public key (any other valid P-256 compressed key).
   158	            var other = Crypto.GenerateP256KeyPair();
   159	            Action act = () =>
   160	            {
   161	                var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(other.PublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
   162	                stamper.SignWithApiKey("payload");
   163	            };
   164	            act.Should().Throw<InvalidOperationException>()
   165	               .WithMessage("Bad API key. Expected to get public key*");
   166	        }
   167	
   168	        [Fact]
   169	        public void Stamp_HeaderConstantsAndScheme()
   170	        {
   171	            ApiKeyStamper.StampHeaderName.Should().Be("X-Stamp");
   172	            ApiKeyStamper.SignatureScheme.Should().Be("SIGNATURE_SCHEME_TK_API_P256");
   173	        }
   174	
   175	        [Fact]
   176	        public void StampHeaderValue_IsAsciiUrlSafe()
   177	        {
   178	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
   179	            var result = stamper.Stamp("hello");
   180	            // base64url alphabet: A-Z a-z 0-9 - _
   181	            foreach (char c in result.StampHeaderValue)
   182	            {
   183	                bool ok = (c >= 'A' && c <= 'Z')
   184	                          || (c >= 'a' && c <= 'z')
   185	                          || (c >= '0' && c <= '9')
   186	                          || c == '-' || c == '_';
   187	                ok.Should().BeTrue($"char '{c}' is not in base64url alphabet");
   188	            }
   189	        }
   190	
   191	        // ============================================================
   192	        // Helpers
   193	        // ============================================================
   194	
   195	        private static byte[] HexToBytes(string hex) => Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(hex);
   196	
   197	        private static void AssertSignatureVerifies(string compressedPubHex, string content, byte[] derSignature)
   198	        {
   199	            byte[] payloadBytes = System.Text.Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(content);
   200	
   201	            var digest = DigestUtilities.GetDigest("SHA-256");
   202	            digest.BlockUpdate(payloadBytes, 0, payloadBytes.Length);
   203	            var hash = new byte[digest.GetDigestSize()];
   204	            digest.DoFinal(hash, 0);
   205	
   206	            var curve = ECNamedCurveTable.GetByName(CryptoConstants.CURVE_NAME);
   207	            var domainParams = new ECDomainParameters(curve.Curve, curve.G, curve.N, curve.H, curve.GetSeed());
   208	
   209	            byte[] compressedPub = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(compressedPubHex);
   210	            var point = curve.Curve.DecodePoint(compressedPub);
   211	            var publicKey = new ECPublicKeyParameters(point, domainParams);
   212	
   213	            var signer = SignerUtilities.GetSigner("NONEwithECDSA");
   214	            signer.Init(false, publicKey);
   215	            signer.BlockUpdate(hash, 0, hash.Length);
   216	            signer.VerifySignature(derSignature).Should().BeTrue();
   217	        }
   218	
   219	        private static (BigInteger R, BigInteger S) ParseDerEcdsa(byte[] der)
   220	        {
   221	            using var ms = new MemoryStream(der);
   222	            using var reader = new Asn1InputStream(ms);
   223	            var seq = (DerSequence)reader.ReadObject();
   224	            var r = ((DerInteger)seq[0]).Value;
   225	            var s = ((DerInteger)seq[1]).Value;
   226	            return (r, s);
   227	        }
   228	    }
   229	}

     1	# Test fixtures
     2	
     3	Each subdirectory groups fixtures for one production file, plus a
     4	provenance note explaining where the bytes came from.
     5	
     6	## Provenance levels
     7	
     8	- **upstream-test-vectors** — directly copied from the upstream
     9	  `__tests__/` directory inside `codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/`.
    10	- **rfc** — standardized test vectors (RFC 5869, RFC 9180, etc.).
    11	- **nist** — NIST CAVP P-256 vectors.
    12	- **turnkey-sample** — sample bundles published by Turnkey in their
    13	  developer docs (de-identified).
    14	- **node-generated** — bytes generated by running the pinned upstream Node
    15	  package inside `tests/Fixtures/Generators/`. The generator script,
    16	  Node version, and output hash are recorded so reviewers can re-run.
    17	
    18	## Layout
    19	
    20	```
    21	Fixtures/
    22	├── encoding/
    23	│   └── turnkey-encoding-vectors.json       — upstream-test-vectors
    24	├── crypto/                                  — (added with Crypto.cs port)
    25	├── api-key-stamper/                         — (added with ApiKeyStamper.cs port)
    26	└── http/                                    — (added with Http.cs port)
    27	```
    28	
    29	## Re-generating
    30	
    31	Fixtures sourced from upstream test files are stable as long as
    32	[`codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/`](../../codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/)
    33	is not bumped. If a pin is bumped, re-extract the upstream `__tests__/`
    34	into the matching fixture file and update its sha256 in
    35	`tarball-checksums.txt` next to the new tarball.

    79	---
    80	name: codex-review
    81	version: 2.1.4
    82	author: BenedictKing
    83	description: "Professional code review skill for Claude Code. Automatically collects file changes and task status. Triggers when working directory has uncommitted changes, or reviews latest commit when clean. Triggers: code review, review, 代码审核, 代码审查, 检查代码"
    84	allowed-tools:
    85	  - Task
    86	  - Bash
    87	  - Read
    88	  - Glob
    89	  - Write
    90	  - Edit
    91	user-invocable: true
    92	---
    93	
    94	# Codex Code Review Skill
    95	
    96	## Trigger Conditions
    97	
    98	Triggered when user input contains:
    99	
   100	- "代码审核", "代码审查", "审查代码", "审核代码"
   101	- "review", "code review", "review code", "codex 审核"
   102	- "帮我审核", "检查代码", "审一下", "看看代码"
   103	
   104	## Core Concept: Intention vs Implementation
   105	
   106	Running `codex review --uncommitted` alone only shows AI "what was done (Implementation)".
   107	Recording intention first tells AI "what you wanted to do (Intention)".
   108	
   109	**"Code changes + intention description" as combined input is the most effective way to improve AI code review quality.**
   110	
   111	## Skill Architecture
   112	
   113	This skill operates in two phases:
   114	
   115	1. **Preparation Phase** (current context): Check working directory, update CHANGELOG
   116	2. **Review Phase** (isolated context): Invoke Task tool to execute Lint + codex review (using context: fork to reduce context waste)
   117	
   118	## Execution Steps
   119	
   120	### 0. [First] Check Working Directory Status
   121	
   122	```bash
   123	git diff --name-only && git status --short
   124	```
   125	
   126	**Decide review mode based on output:**
   127	
   128	- **Has uncommitted changes** → Continue with steps 1-4 (normal flow)
   129	- **Clean working directory** → Directly invoke codex-runner: `codex review --commit HEAD`
   130	
   131	### 1. [Mandatory] Check if CHANGELOG is Updated
   132	
   133	**Before any review, must check if CHANGELOG.md contains description of current changes.**
   134	
   135	```bash
   136	# Check if CHANGELOG.md is in uncommitted changes
   137	git diff --name-only | grep -E "(CHANGELOG|changelog)"
   138	```
   139	
   140	**If CHANGELOG is not updated, you must automatically perform the following (don't ask user to do it manually):**
   141	
   142	1. **Analyze changes**: Run `git diff --stat` and `git diff` to get complete changes
   143	2. **Auto-generate CHANGELOG entry**: Generate compliant entry based on code changes
   144	3. **Write to CHANGELOG.md**: Use Edit tool to insert entry at top of `[Unreleased]` section
   145	4. **Continue review flow**: Immediately proceed to next steps after CHANGELOG update
   146	
   147	**Auto-generated CHANGELOG entry format:**
   148	
   149	```markdown
   150	## [Unreleased]
   151	
   152	### Added / Changed / Fixed
   153	
   154	- Feature description: what problem was solved or what functionality was implemented
   155	- Affected files: main modified files/modules
   156	```
   157	
   158	**Example - Auto-generation Flow:**
   159	
   160	```
   161	1. Detected CHANGELOG not updated
   162	2. Run git diff --stat, found handlers/responses.go modified (+88 lines)
   163	3. Run git diff to analyze details: added CompactHandler function
   164	4. Auto-generate entry:
   165	   ### Added
   166	   - Added `/v1/responses/compact` endpoint for conversation context compression
   167	   - Supports multi-channel failover and request body size limits
   168	5. Use Edit tool to write to CHANGELOG.md
   169	6. Continue with lint and codex review
   170	```
   171	
   172	### 2. [Critical] Stage All New Files
   173	
   174	**Before invoking codex review, must add all new files (untracked files) to git staging area, otherwise codex will report P1 error.**
   175	
   176	```bash
   177	# Check for new files
   178	git status --short | grep "^??"
   179	```
   180	
   181	**If there are new files, automatically execute:**
   182	
   183	```bash
   184	# Safely stage all new files (handles empty list and special filenames)
   185	git ls-files --others --exclude-standard -z | while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do git add -- "$f"; done
   186	```
   187	
   188	**Explanation:**
   189	
   190	- `-z` uses null character to separate filenames, correctly handles filenames with spaces/newlines
   191	- `while IFS= read -r -d ''` reads filenames one by one
   192	- `git add -- "$f"` uses `--` separator, correctly handles filenames starting with `-`
   193	- When no new files exist, loop body doesn't execute, safely skipped
   194	- This won't stage modified files, only handles new files
   195	- codex needs files to be tracked by git for proper review
   196	
   197	### 3. Evaluate Task Difficulty and Invoke codex-runner
   198	
   199	**Count change scale:**
   200	
   201	```bash
   202	# Count number of changed files and lines of code
   203	git diff --stat | tail -1
   204	```
   205	
   206	**Difficulty Assessment Criteria:**
   207	
   208	**Difficult Tasks** (meets any condition):
   209	
   210	- Modified files ≥ 10
   211	- Total code changes (insertions + deletions) ≥ 500 lines
   212	- Single metric: insertions ≥ 300 lines OR deletions ≥ 300 lines
   213	- Involves core architecture/algorithm changes
   214	- Cross-module refactoring
   215	- Config: `model_reasoning_effort=xhigh`, timeout 30 minutes
   216	
   217	**Normal Tasks** (other cases):
   218	
   219	- Config: `model_reasoning_effort=high`, timeout 10 minutes
   220	
   221	**Evaluation Method:**
   222	
   223	You MUST parse the `git diff --stat` output correctly to determine difficulty:
   224	
   225	```bash
   226	# Get the summary line (last line of git diff --stat)
   227	git diff --stat | tail -1
   228	# Example outputs:
   229	# "20 files changed, 342 insertions(+), 985 deletions(-)"
   230	# "1 file changed, 50 insertions(+)"  # No deletions
   231	# "3 files changed, 120 deletions(-)"  # No insertions
   232	```
   233	
   234	**Parsing Rules:**
   235	1. Extract file count from "X file(s) changed" (handle both "1 file" and "N files")
   236	2. Extract insertions from "Y insertion(s)(+)" if present (handle both "1 insertion" and "N insertions"), otherwise 0
   237	3. Extract deletions from "Z deletion(s)(-)" if present (handle both "1 deletion" and "N deletions"), otherwise 0
   238	4. Calculate total changes = insertions + deletions
   239	
   240	**Important Edge Cases:**
   241	- Single file: `"1 file changed"` (singular form)
   242	- No insertions: Git omits `"insertions(+)"` entirely → treat as 0
   243	- No deletions: Git omits `"deletions(-)"` entirely → treat as 0
   244	- Pure rename: May show `"0 insertions(+), 0 deletions(-)"` or omit both
   245	
   246	**Decision Logic (ANY condition triggers xhigh):**
   247	- IF file_count >= 10 → xhigh
   248	- IF total_changes >= 500 → xhigh
   249	- IF insertions >= 300 → xhigh
   250	- IF deletions >= 300 → xhigh
   251	- ELSE → high
   252	
   253	**Example Cases:**
   254	- ✅ "20 files changed, 342 insertions(+), 985 deletions(-)" → xhigh (files=20≥10, total=1327≥500, deletions=985≥300)
   255	- ✅ "5 files changed, 600 insertions(+), 50 deletions(-)" → xhigh (total=650≥500, insertions=600≥300)
   256	- ✅ "12 files changed, 100 insertions(+), 50 deletions(-)" → xhigh (files=12≥10)
   257	- ✅ "1 file changed, 400 deletions(-)" → xhigh (deletions=400≥300)
   258	- ❌ "3 files changed, 150 insertions(+), 80 deletions(-)" → high (all conditions fail)
   259	- ❌ "1 file changed, 50 insertions(+)" → high (no deletions, total=50<500)
   260	
   261	**Invoke codex-runner Subtask:**
   262	
   263	Use Task tool to invoke codex-runner, passing complete command (including Lint + codex review):
   264	
   265	```
   266	Task parameters:
   267	- subagent_type: Bash
   268	- description: "Execute Lint and codex review"
   269	- timeout: 1800000 (30 minutes for difficult tasks) or 600000 (10 minutes for normal tasks)
   270	- prompt: Choose corresponding command based on project type and difficulty
   271	
   272	Go project - Difficult task:
   273	  go fmt ./... && go vet ./... && codex review --uncommitted --config model_reasoning_effort=xhigh
   274	  (timeout: 1800000)
   275	
   276	Go project - Normal task:
   277	  go fmt ./... && go vet ./... && codex review --uncommitted --config model_reasoning_effort=high
   278	  (timeout: 600000)
   279	
   280	Node project - Difficult task:
   281	  npm run lint:fix && codex review --uncommitted --config model_reasoning_effort=xhigh
   282	  (timeout: 1800000)
   283	
   284	Node project - Normal task:
   285	  npm run lint:fix && codex review --uncommitted --config model_reasoning_effort=high
   286	  (timeout: 600000)
   287	
   288	Python project - Difficult task:
   289	  black . && ruff check --fix . && codex review --uncommitted --config model_reasoning_effort=xhigh
   290	  (timeout: 1800000)
   291	
   292	Python project - Normal task:
   293	  black . && ruff check --fix . && codex review --uncommitted --config model_reasoning_effort=high
   294	  (timeout: 600000)
   295	
   296	Clean working directory:
   297	  codex review --commit HEAD --config model_reasoning_effort=high
   298	  (timeout: 600000)
   299

   327	     5	//
   328	     6	// Files covered:
   329	     7	//   ts-source/index.ts                -> ApiKeyStamper class + signWithApiKey

   331	     9	//   ts-source/purejs.ts               -> signWithApiKey "purejs" runtime
   332	    10	//                                         (P-256 ECDSA via @noble/curves,
   333	    11	//                                          SHA-256, DER hex output)
   334	    12	//
   335	    13	// Runtime selection note:
   336	    14	//   The upstream package supports three runtimes — "browser" (WebCrypto),
   337	    15	//   "node" (Node crypto), "purejs" (@noble/curves). This C# port mirrors
   338	    16	//   the **purejs** runtime since BouncyCastle exposes the same primitives
   339	    17	//   in a single backend. There is no runtime detection in C#; behavior is
   340	    18	//   identical to what upstream produces when `runtimeOverride = "purejs"`
   341	    19	//   is passed.
   342	    20	//
   343	    21	// Wire bytes:
   344	    22	//   - signature.toDERHex() output equals upstream's noble signature.toDERHex().
   345	    23	//   - The stamp JSON shape is exactly the upstream's
   346	    24	//     { publicKey, scheme, signature } in this order.
   347	    25	//   - The X-Stamp header value is base64url(JSON), matching upstream's
   348	    26	//     `stringToBase64urlString(JSON.stringify(stamp))`.
   349	    27	//   - SHA-256(content) and deterministic ECDSA (RFC 6979) are used; low-S is
   350	    28	//     enforced (noble defaults to lowS: true).
   351	    29	
   352	    30	using System;
   353	    31	using System.IO;
   354	    32	using Org.BouncyCastle.Asn1.X9;
   355	    33	using Org.BouncyCastle.Crypto.Parameters;

   358	    36	using Org.BouncyCastle.Security;
   359	    37	
   360	    38	namespace Turnkey
   361	    39	{
   362	    40	    /// <summary>
   363	    41	    /// Stamper for Turnkey API requests. 1:1 logical port of upstream
   364	    42	    /// <c>@turnkey/api-key-stamper</c> at peak's pinned version 0.5.0.
   365	    43	    /// </summary>
   366	    44	    public class ApiKeyStamper
   367	    45	    {
   368	    46	        /// <summary>Header name used by Turnkey API requests.</summary>
   369	    47	        public const string StampHeaderName = "X-Stamp";
   370	    48	
   371	    49	        /// <summary>Turnkey signature scheme identifier for P-256 API keys.</summary>
   372	    50	        public const string SignatureScheme = "SIGNATURE_SCHEME_TK_API_P256";
   373	    51	
   374	    52	        private readonly string _apiPublicKey;
   375	    53	        private readonly string _apiPrivateKey;
   376	    54	        private readonly BigInteger _privateKeyScalar;
   377	    55	        private readonly ECDomainParameters _domainParams;
   378	    56	
   379	    57	        /// <summary>
   380	    58	        /// Initialize the stamper with a P-256 API key pair.
   381	    59	        /// </summary>
   382	    60	        /// <param name="apiPublicKey">Compressed P-256 public key as hex (66 chars).</param>
   383	    61	        /// <param name="apiPrivateKey">P-256 private key scalar as 64 hex chars.</param>
   384	    62	        public ApiKeyStamper(string apiPublicKey, string apiPrivateKey)
   385	    63	        {
   386	    64	            _apiPublicKey = apiPublicKey ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(apiPublicKey));
   387	    65	            _apiPrivateKey = apiPrivateKey ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(apiPrivateKey));
   388	    66	
   389	    67	            var curve = ECNamedCurveTable.GetByName(CryptoConstants.CURVE_NAME);
   390	    68	            _domainParams = new ECDomainParameters(curve.Curve, curve.G, curve.N, curve.H, curve.GetSeed());
   391	    69	
   392	    70	            // Parse + validate private key against the noble invariants
   393	    71	            // (exactly 32 bytes, scalar in [1, n - 1]).
   394	    72	            var privateKeyBytes = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(_apiPrivateKey);
   395	    73	            if (privateKeyBytes.Length != 32)
   396	    74	            {
   397	    75	                throw new ArgumentException(
   398	    76	                    "invalid P-256 private key: expected 32 bytes, got " + privateKeyBytes.Length);
   399	    77	            }
   400	    78	            _privateKeyScalar = new BigInteger(1, privateKeyBytes);
   401	    79	            if (_privateKeyScalar.SignValue == 0 || _privateKeyScalar.CompareTo(_domainParams.N) >= 0)
   402	    80	            {
   403	    81	                throw new ArgumentException(
   404	    82	                    "invalid P-256 private key: scalar must be in [1, n - 1]");
   405	    83	            }
   406	    84	        }
   407	    85	
   408	    86	        /// <summary>
   409	    87	        /// Output of <see cref="Stamp(string)"/>: the header name and the
   410	    88	        /// base64url-encoded stamp JSON to use as the header value.
   411	    89	        /// </summary>
   412	    90	        public class StampResult
   413	    91	        {
   414	    92	            public string StampHeaderName { get; set; } = string.Empty;
   415	    93	            public string StampHeaderValue { get; set; } = string.Empty;
   416	    94	        }
   417	    95	
   418	    96	        /// <summary>
   419	    97	        /// Internal JSON shape of the stamp. Field order matches upstream
   420	    98	        /// (<c>publicKey, scheme, signature</c>) so the base64url-encoded
   421	    99	        /// header value is wire-identical to what
   422	   100	        /// <c>JSON.stringify({ publicKey, scheme, signature })</c> produces.
   423	   101	        /// </summary>
   424	   102	        public class TurnkeyStamp
   425	   103	        {
   426	   104	            [System.Text.Json.Serialization.JsonPropertyName("publicKey")]
   427	   105	            public string PublicKey { get; set; } = string.Empty;
   428	   106	
   429	   107	            [System.Text.Json.Serialization.JsonPropertyName("scheme")]
   430	   108	            public string Scheme { get; set; } = string.Empty;
   431	   109	
   432	   110	            [System.Text.Json.Serialization.JsonPropertyName("signature")]
   433	   111	            public string Signature { get; set; } = string.Empty;
   434	   112	        }
   435	   113	
   436	   114	        /// <summary>
   437	   115	        /// Produce a Turnkey API stamp for <paramref name="payload"/>.
   438	   116	        /// Equivalent to upstream <c>ApiKeyStamper.stamp(payload)</c>.
   439	   117	        /// </summary>
   440	   118	        public StampResult Stamp(string payload)
   441	   119	        {
   442	   120	            if (payload == null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(payload));
   443	   121	
   444	   122	            string signatureDerHex = SignWithApiKey(payload);
   445	   123	
   446	   124	            var stamp = new TurnkeyStamp
   447	   125	            {
   448	   126	                PublicKey = _apiPublicKey,
   449	   127	                Scheme = SignatureScheme,
   450	   128	                Signature = signatureDerHex,
   451	   129	            };
   452	   130	
   453	   131	            string stampJson = System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer.Serialize(
   454	   132	                stamp, TurnkeyJsonContext.Default.TurnkeyStamp);
   455	   133	
   456	   134	            return new StampResult
   457	   135	            {
   458	   136	                StampHeaderName = StampHeaderName,
   459	   137	                StampHeaderValue = Encoding.StringToBase64UrlString(stampJson),
   460	   138	            };
   461	   139	        }
   462	   140	
   463	   141	        /// <summary>
   464	   142	        /// Mirror of upstream <c>purejs.ts signWithApiKey</c>. Verifies that
   465	   143	        /// the configured public key matches the one derived from the
   466	   144	        /// configured private key, then SHA-256-hashes the payload and signs
   467	   145	        /// it with deterministic ECDSA (RFC 6979 + low-S) returning the
   468	   146	        /// DER-encoded signature as a hex string.
   469	   147	        /// </summary>
   470	   148	        public string SignWithApiKey(string content)
   471	   149	        {
   472	   150	            if (content == null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(content));
   473	   151	
   474	   152	            // Upstream:
   475	   153	            //   const publicKey = p256.getPublicKey(input.privateKey, true);
   476	   154	            //   const publicKeyString = uint8ArrayToHexString(publicKey);
   477	   155	            //   if (publicKeyString != input.publicKey) throw new Error(...);
   478	   156	            byte[] derivedPublicKeyBytes = Crypto.GetPublicKey(_apiPrivateKey, isCompressed: true);
   479	   157	            string derivedPublicKey = Encoding.Uint8ArrayToHexString(derivedPublicKeyBytes);
   480	   158	            if (!string.Equals(derivedPublicKey, _apiPublicKey, StringComparison.Ordinal))
   481	   159	            {
   482	   160	                throw new InvalidOperationException(
   483	   161	                    "Bad API key. Expected to get public key " + _apiPublicKey
   484	   162	                    + ", got " + derivedPublicKey);
   485	   163	            }
   486	   164	
   487	   165	            // Upstream:
   488	   166	            //   const hash = createHash().update(input.content).digest();   // SHA-256
   489	   167	            //   const signature = p256.sign(hash, input.privateKey);        // RFC 6979 + lowS
   490	   168	            //   return signature.toDERHex();
   491	   169	            byte[] payloadBytes = System.Text.Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(content);
   492	   170	
   493	   171	            var digest = DigestUtilities.GetDigest("SHA-256");
   494	   172	            digest.BlockUpdate(payloadBytes, 0, payloadBytes.Length);
   495	   173	            var hash = new byte[digest.GetDigestSize()];
   496	   174	            digest.DoFinal(hash, 0);
   497	   175	
   498	   176	            var hmacCalculator = new HMacDsaKCalculator(DigestUtilities.GetDigest("SHA-256"));
   499	   177	            var signer = new ECDsaSigner(hmacCalculator);
   500	   178	            signer.Init(true, new ECPrivateKeyParameters(_privateKeyScalar, _domainParams));
   501	   179	
   502	   180	            BigInteger[] signature = signer.GenerateSignature(hash);
   503	   181	            BigInteger r = signature[0];
   504	   182	            BigInteger s = signature[1];
   505	   183	
   506	   184	            // Low-S normalization (noble enables lowS by default).
   507	   185	            BigInteger halfN = _domainParams.N.ShiftRight(1);
   508	   186	            if (s.CompareTo(halfN) > 0)
   509	   187	            {
   510	   188	                s = _domainParams.N.Subtract(s);
   511	   189	            }
   512	   190	
   513	   191	            return EncodeDerSignatureHex(r, s);
   514	   192	        }
   515	   193	
   516	   194	        /// <summary>
   517	   195	        /// Encode the <c>(r, s)</c> pair as a DER ASN.1 SEQUENCE of two
   518	   196	        /// INTEGERs, then hex-encode the result.
   519	   197	        /// Equivalent to noble's <c>signature.toDERHex()</c>.
   520	   198	        /// </summary>
   521	   199	        private static string EncodeDerSignatureHex(BigInteger r, BigInteger s)
   522	   200	        {
   523	   201	            byte[] rBytes = r.ToByteArrayUnsigned();
   524	   202	            byte[] sBytes = s.ToByteArrayUnsigned();
   525	   203	
   526	   204	            bool rNeedsPadding = rBytes.Length > 0 && (rBytes[0] & 0x80) != 0;
   527	   205	            bool sNeedsPadding = sBytes.Length > 0 && (sBytes[0] & 0x80) != 0;
   528	   206	            int rLen = rBytes.Length + (rNeedsPadding ? 1 : 0);
   529	   207	            int sLen = sBytes.Length + (sNeedsPadding ? 1 : 0);
   530	   208	
   531	   209	            int contentLen = 2 + rLen + 2 + sLen; // (INTEGER tag + length) * 2 + r + s
   532	   210	
   533	   211	            using (var ms = new MemoryStream())
   534	   212	            {
   535	   213	                ms.WriteByte(0x30); // SEQUENCE
   536	   214	
   537	   215	                if (contentLen >= 128)
   538	   216	                {
   539	   217	                    // Long-form length (1 octet count). For ECDSA P-256
   540	   218	                    // signatures the total length never exceeds 0x7f in
   541	   219	                    // practice; this branch is defensive.
   542	   220	                    ms.WriteByte(0x81);
   543	   221	                    ms.WriteByte((byte)contentLen);
   544	   222	                }
   545	   223	                else
   546	   224	                {
   547	   225	                    ms.WriteByte((byte)contentLen);
   548	   226	                }
   549	   227	
   550	   228	                ms.WriteByte(0x02); // INTEGER tag (r)
   551	   229	                ms.WriteByte((byte)rLen);
   552	   230	                if (rNeedsPadding) ms.WriteByte(0x00);
   553	   231	                ms.Write(rBytes, 0, rBytes.Length);
   554	   232	
   555	   233	                ms.WriteByte(0x02); // INTEGER tag (s)
   556	   234	                ms.WriteByte((byte)sLen);
   557	   235	                if (sNeedsPadding) ms.WriteByte(0x00);
   558	   236	                ms.Write(sBytes, 0, sBytes.Length);
   559	   237	
   560	   238	                return Encoding.Uint8ArrayToHexString(ms.ToArray());
   561	   239	            }
   562	   240	        }
   563	   241	    }
   564	   242	}

   741	     4	// Header name for an API key stamp
   742	     5	const stampHeaderName = "X-Stamp";
   743	     6	
   744	     7	export type Runtime = "browser" | "node" | "purejs";
   745	     8	
   746	     9	export type TApiKeyStamperConfig = {
   747	    10	  apiPublicKey: string;
   748	    11	  apiPrivateKey: string;
   749	    12	  runtimeOverride?: Runtime | undefined;
   750	    13	};
   751	    14	
   752	    15	// `window.document` ensures that we're in a browser context
   753	    16	// and `crypto.subtle` ensures that it supports the web crypto APIs
   754	    17	// Inspired by https://github.com/flexdinesh/browser-or-node/blob/master/src/index.ts
   755	    18	const isCryptoEnabledBrowser: boolean =
   756	    19	  typeof window !== "undefined" &&
   757	    20	  typeof window.document !== "undefined" &&
   758	    21	  typeof crypto !== "undefined" &&
   759	    22	  typeof crypto.subtle !== "undefined";
   760	    23	
   761	    24	// We check `process.versions.node`
   762	    25	// Taken from https://github.com/flexdinesh/browser-or-node/blob/master/src/index.ts
   763	    26	const isNode: boolean =
   764	    27	  typeof process !== "undefined" &&
   765	    28	  process.versions != null &&
   766	    29	  process.versions.node != null;
   767	    30	
   768	    31	const detectRuntime = (): Runtime => {
   769	    32	  if (isCryptoEnabledBrowser) {
   770	    33	    return "browser";
   771	    34	  }
   772	    35	
   773	    36	  if (isNode) {
   774	    37	    return "node";
   775	    38	  }
   776	    39	
   777	    40	  // If we don't have NodeJS or web crypto at our disposal, default to pure JS implementation
   778	    41	  // This is the case for old browsers and react native environments
   779	    42	  return "purejs";
   780	    43	};
   781	    44	
   782	    45	/**
   783	    46	 * Signature function abstracting the differences between NodeJS and web environments for signing with API keys.
   784	    47	 */
   785	    48	export const signWithApiKey = async (
   786	    49	  input: {
   787	    50	    content: string;
   788	    51	    publicKey: string;
   789	    52	    privateKey: string;
   790	    53	  },
   791	    54	  runtimeOverride?: Runtime | undefined,
   792	    55	): Promise<string> => {
   793	    56	  const runtime = runtimeOverride ?? detectRuntime();
   794	    57	
   795	    58	  switch (runtime) {
   796	    59	    case "browser":
   797	    60	      return (await import("./webcrypto")).signWithApiKey(input);
   798	    61	    case "node":
   799	    62	      return (await import("./nodecrypto")).signWithApiKey(input);
   800	    63	    case "purejs":
   801	    64	      return (await import("./purejs")).signWithApiKey(input);
   802	    65	    default:
   803	    66	      throw new Error(`Unsupported runtime: ${runtime}`);
   804	    67	  }
   805	    68	};
   806	    69	
   807	    70	/**
   808	    71	 * Stamper to use with `@turnkey/http`'s `TurnkeyClient`
   809	    72	 */
   810	    73	export class ApiKeyStamper {
   811	    74	  apiPublicKey: string;
   812	    75	  apiPrivateKey: string;
   813	    76	  runtimeOverride?: Runtime | undefined;
   814	    77	
   815	    78	  constructor(config: TApiKeyStamperConfig) {
   816	    79	    this.apiPublicKey = config.apiPublicKey;
   817	    80	    this.apiPrivateKey = config.apiPrivateKey;
   818	    81	    this.runtimeOverride = config.runtimeOverride;
   819	    82	  }
   820	    83	
   821	    84	  async stamp(payload: string) {
   822	    85	    const signature = await signWithApiKey(
   823	    86	      {
   824	    87	        publicKey: this.apiPublicKey,
   825	    88	        privateKey: this.apiPrivateKey,
   826	    89	        content: payload,
   827	    90	      },
   828	    91	      this.runtimeOverride,
   829	    92	    );
   830	    93	
   831	    94	    const stamp = {
   832	    95	      publicKey: this.apiPublicKey,
   833	    96	      scheme: "SIGNATURE_SCHEME_TK_API_P256",
   834	    97	      signature,
   835	    98	    };
   836	    99	
   837	   100	    return {
   838	   101	      stampHeaderName,
   839	   102	      stampHeaderValue: stringToBase64urlString(JSON.stringify(stamp)),
   840	   103	    };
   841	   104	  }
   842	   105	}

   850	     2	import { createHash } from "sha256-uint8array";
   851	     3	import { uint8ArrayToHexString } from "@turnkey/encoding";
   852	     4	
   853	     5	export const signWithApiKey = async (input: {
   854	     6	  content: string;
   855	     7	  publicKey: string;
   856	     8	  privateKey: string;
   857	     9	}): Promise<string> => {
   858	    10	  const publicKey = p256.getPublicKey(input.privateKey, true);
   859	    11	
   860	    12	  // Public key in the usual 02 or 03 + 64 hex digits
   861	    13	  const publicKeyString = uint8ArrayToHexString(publicKey);
   862	    14

   864	    16	    throw new Error(
   865	    17	      `Bad API key. Expected to get public key ${input.publicKey}, got ${publicKeyString}`,
   866	    18	    );
   867	    19	  }

   870	    22	  const signature = p256.sign(hash, input.privateKey);
   871	    23	  return signature.toDERHex();
   872	    24	};

   884	     8	/**
   885	     9	 * Converts a Turnkey API key pair into a JSON Web Key (JWK) format.
   886	    10	 * This function accepts P-256 API keys only.
   887	    11	 *
   888	    12	 * @param {Object} input - The Turnkey API key components.
   889	    13	 * @param {string} input.uncompressedPrivateKeyHex - Hexadecimal-encoded uncompressed private key (32-byte scalar).
   890	    14	 * @param {string} input.compressedPublicKeyHex - Hexadecimal-encoded compressed public key (33 bytes).
   891	    15	 * @returns {JsonWebKey} A JSON Web Key object representing the EC P-256 key.
   892	    16	 */
   893	    17	export function convertTurnkeyApiKeyToJwk(input: {
   894	    18	  uncompressedPrivateKeyHex: string;
   895	    19	  compressedPublicKeyHex: string;
   896	    20	}): JsonWebKey {
   897	    21	  const { uncompressedPrivateKeyHex, compressedPublicKeyHex } = input;
   898	    22	
   899	    23	  let jwk;
   900	    24	  try {
   901	    25	    jwk = pointDecode(uint8ArrayFromHexString(compressedPublicKeyHex));
   902	    26	  } catch (e) {
   903	    27	    throw new Error(
   904	    28	      `unable to load API key: invalid public key. Did you switch your public and private key by accident? Is your public key a valid, compressed P-256 public key?`,
   905	    29	    );
   906	    30	  }
   907	    31	
   908	    32	  // Ensure that d is sufficiently padded
   909	    33	  jwk.d = hexStringToBase64url(
   910	    34	    uncompressedPrivateKeyHex,
   911	    35	    DEFAULT_JWK_MEMBER_BYTE_LENGTH,
   912	    36	  );
   913	    37	
   914	    38	  return jwk;
   915	    39	}

   920	     1	// ApiKeyStamperTests.cs — xunit tests for src/ApiKeyStamper.cs.
   921	     2	//

   923	     4	//   codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-api-key-stamper-0.5.0/ts-source/__fixtures__/api-key.{private,public}
   924	     5	//   codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-api-key-stamper-0.5.0/ts-source/__tests__/stamp-test.ts
   925	     6	//
   926	     7	// P-256 ECDSA signatures are not deterministic across libraries (noble uses
   927	     8	// RFC 6979; BouncyCastle's HMacDsaKCalculator also uses RFC 6979 with the
   928	     9	// same digest, so for identical input + key + nonce-source the signature
   929	    10	// is deterministic). Tests therefore assert:
   930	    11	//   - the header name == "X-Stamp"
   931	    12	//   - the base64url-decoded JSON has keys ["publicKey", "scheme", "signature"]
   932	    13	//   - publicKey matches the configured key
   933	    14	//   - scheme == "SIGNATURE_SCHEME_TK_API_P256"
   934	    15	//   - the signature is a valid DER-encoded ECDSA signature over SHA-256(content)
   935	    16	//     under the configured public key
   936	    17	
   937	    18	using System;
   938	    19	using System.IO;
   939	    20	using System.Text;
   940	    21	using System.Text.Json;
   941	    22	using FluentAssertions;
   942	    23	using Org.BouncyCastle.Asn1;
   943	    24	using Org.BouncyCastle.Asn1.X9;
   944	    25	using Org.BouncyCastle.Crypto.Parameters;
   945	    26	using Org.BouncyCastle.Math;
