   947	    28	using Xunit;
   948	    29	
   949	    30	namespace Turnkey.Tests
   950	    31	{
   951	    32	    public class ApiKeyStamperTests
   952	    33	    {
   953	    34	        // From upstream __fixtures__/api-key.{private,public}
   954	    35	        private const string FixturePrivateKey =
   955	    36	            "487f361ddfd73440e707f4daa6775b376859e8a3c9f29b3bb694a12927c0213c";
   956	    37	        private const string FixturePublicKey =
   957	    38	            "02f739f8c77b32f4d5f13265861febd76e7a9c61a1140d296b8c16302508870316";
   958	    39	
   959	    40	        [Fact]
   960	    41	        public void Constructor_RejectsBadKeyLength()
   961	    42	        {
   962	    43	            // Private key not exactly 32 bytes -> reject
   963	    44	            Action act = () => new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, "abcd");
   964	    45	            act.Should().Throw<ArgumentException>()
   965	    46	               .WithMessage("invalid P-256 private key: expected 32 bytes, got 2");
   966	    47	        }
   967	    48	
   968	    49	        [Fact]
   969	    50	        public void Constructor_RejectsZeroScalar()
   970	    51	        {
   971	    52	            string zero = new string('0', 64);
   972	    53	            Action act = () => new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, zero);
   973	    54	            act.Should().Throw<ArgumentException>()
   974	    55	               .WithMessage("invalid P-256 private key: scalar must be in [1, n - 1]");
   975	    56	        }
   976	    57	
   977	    58	        [Fact]
   978	    59	        public void Stamp_UpstreamFixture_ProducesValidWireBytes()
   979	    60	        {
   980	    61	            // Matches upstream __tests__/stamp-test.ts:6-37
   981	    62	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
   982	    63	            const string messageToSign = "hello from TKHQ!";
   983	    64	
   984	    65	            var result = stamper.Stamp(messageToSign);
   985	    66	
   986	    67	            result.StampHeaderName.Should().Be("X-Stamp");
   987	    68	
   988	    69	            // Decode base64url -> JSON -> object.
   989	    70	            string decodedJson = Encoding.DecodeBase64UrlToString(result.StampHeaderValue);
   990	    71	
   991	    72	            using var doc = JsonDocument.Parse(decodedJson);
   992	    73	            var root = doc.RootElement;
   993	    74	
   994	    75	            // Keys must be exactly publicKey, scheme, signature in this order.
   995	    76	            int i = 0;
   996	    77	            string[] expectedKeys = { "publicKey", "scheme", "signature" };
   997	    78	            foreach (var prop in root.EnumerateObject())
   998	    79	            {
   999	    80	                prop.Name.Should().Be(expectedKeys[i++]);
  1000	    81	            }
  1001	    82	            i.Should().Be(3);
  1002	    83	
  1003	    84	            root.GetProperty("publicKey").GetString().Should().Be(FixturePublicKey);
  1004	    85	            root.GetProperty("scheme").GetString().Should().Be("SIGNATURE_SCHEME_TK_API_P256");
  1005	    86	
  1006	    87	            // Verify the DER ECDSA signature against SHA-256(content) under the
  1007	    88	            // public key. This is the wire-format gate: if the signature isn't
  1008	    89	            // verifiable against the configured P-256 key, the stamp would be
  1009	    90	            // rejected by Turnkey's backend.
  1010	    91	            string signatureHex = root.GetProperty("signature").GetString()!;
  1011	    92	            byte[] signatureBytes = HexToBytes(signatureHex);
  1012	    93	            AssertSignatureVerifies(FixturePublicKey, messageToSign, signatureBytes);
  1013	    94	        }
  1014	    95	
  1015	    96	        [Fact]
  1016	    97	        public void SignWithApiKey_ProducesDerHexThatVerifies()
  1017	    98	        {
  1018	    99	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
  1019	   100	            const string content = "any payload we care to sign";
  1020	   101	            string sigHex = stamper.SignWithApiKey(content);
  1021	   102	
  1022	   103	            // Output is DER hex; even length, starts with 30 (SEQUENCE tag).
  1023	   104	            (sigHex.Length % 2).Should().Be(0);
  1024	   105	            sigHex.Substring(0, 2).Should().Be("30");
  1025	   106	
  1026	   107	            AssertSignatureVerifies(FixturePublicKey, content, HexToBytes(sigHex));
  1027	   108	        }
  1028	   109	
  1029	   110	        [Fact]
  1030	   111	        public void SignWithApiKey_DeterministicWithRfc6979()
  1031	   112	        {
  1032	   113	            // RFC 6979 + same key + same content + same SHA-256(content) +
  1033	   114	            // same low-S normalization => signature is bit-identical across
  1034	   115	            // invocations (HMacDsaKCalculator is deterministic).
  1035	   116	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
  1036	   117	            string a = stamper.SignWithApiKey("the same content");
  1037	   118	            string b = stamper.SignWithApiKey("the same content");
  1038	   119	            a.Should().Be(b);
  1039	   120	        }
  1040	   121	
  1041	   122	        [Fact]
  1042	   123	        public void SignWithApiKey_LowSEnforced()
  1043	   124	        {
  1044	   125	            // For RFC 6979 + low-S: the s value's high bit (top byte) must be
  1045	   126	            // strictly less than (n / 2 + 1). Verify by parsing the DER and
  1046	   127	            // checking the s integer is in the lower half of [1, n-1].
  1047	   128	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
  1048	   129	            for (int i = 0; i < 8; i++)
  1049	   130	            {
  1050	   131	                string sigHex = stamper.SignWithApiKey("content_" + i);
  1051	   132	                var (r, s) = ParseDerEcdsa(HexToBytes(sigHex));
  1052	   133	
  1053	   134	                var curve = ECNamedCurveTable.GetByName(CryptoConstants.CURVE_NAME);
  1054	   135	                var halfN = curve.N.ShiftRight(1);
  1055	   136	                s.CompareTo(halfN).Should().BeLessOrEqualTo(0,
  1056	   137	                    "low-S requires s to be in (0, n/2]");
  1057	   138	                r.SignValue.Should().BeGreaterThan(0);
  1058	   139	                s.SignValue.Should().BeGreaterThan(0);
  1059	   140	            }
  1060	   141	        }
  1061	   142	
  1062	   143	        [Fact]
  1063	   144	        public void SignWithApiKey_WrongPublicKey_Throws()
  1064	   145	        {
  1065	   146	            // Mismatched public key (any other valid P-256 compressed key).
  1066	   147	            var other = Crypto.GenerateP256KeyPair();
  1067	   148	            Action act = () =>
  1068	   149	            {
  1069	   150	                var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(other.PublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
  1070	   151	                stamper.SignWithApiKey("payload");
  1071	   152	            };
  1072	   153	            act.Should().Throw<InvalidOperationException>()
  1073	   154	               .WithMessage("Bad API key. Expected to get public key*");
  1074	   155	        }
  1075	   156	
  1076	   157	        [Fact]
  1077	   158	        public void Stamp_HeaderConstantsAndScheme()
  1078	   159	        {
  1079	   160	            ApiKeyStamper.StampHeaderName.Should().Be("X-Stamp");
  1080	   161	            ApiKeyStamper.SignatureScheme.Should().Be("SIGNATURE_SCHEME_TK_API_P256");
  1081	   162	        }
  1082	   163	
  1083	   164	        [Fact]
  1084	   165	        public void StampHeaderValue_IsAsciiUrlSafe()
  1085	   166	        {
  1086	   167	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
  1087	   168	            var result = stamper.Stamp("hello");
  1088	   169	            // base64url alphabet: A-Z a-z 0-9 - _
  1089	   170	            foreach (char c in result.StampHeaderValue)
  1090	   171	            {
  1091	   172	                bool ok = (c >= 'A' && c <= 'Z')
  1092	   173	                          || (c >= 'a' && c <= 'z')
  1093	   174	                          || (c >= '0' && c <= '9')
  1094	   175	                          || c == '-' || c == '_';
  1095	   176	                ok.Should().BeTrue($"char '{c}' is not in base64url alphabet");
  1096	   177	            }
  1097	   178	        }
  1098	   179	
  1099	   180	        // ============================================================
  1100	   181	        // Helpers
  1101	   182	        // ============================================================
  1102	   183	
  1103	   184	        private static byte[] HexToBytes(string hex) => Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(hex);
  1104	   185	
  1105	   186	        private static void AssertSignatureVerifies(string compressedPubHex, string content, byte[] derSignature)
  1106	   187	        {
  1107	   188	            byte[] payloadBytes = System.Text.Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(content);
  1108	   189	
  1109	   190	            var digest = DigestUtilities.GetDigest("SHA-256");
  1110	   191	            digest.BlockUpdate(payloadBytes, 0, payloadBytes.Length);
  1111	   192	            var hash = new byte[digest.GetDigestSize()];
  1112	   193	            digest.DoFinal(hash, 0);
  1113	   194	
  1114	   195	            var curve = ECNamedCurveTable.GetByName(CryptoConstants.CURVE_NAME);
  1115	   196	            var domainParams = new ECDomainParameters(curve.Curve, curve.G, curve.N, curve.H, curve.GetSeed());
  1116	   197	
  1117	   198	            byte[] compressedPub = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(compressedPubHex);
  1118	   199	            var point = curve.Curve.DecodePoint(compressedPub);
  1119	   200	            var publicKey = new ECPublicKeyParameters(point, domainParams);
  1120	   201	
  1121	   202	            var signer = SignerUtilities.GetSigner("NONEwithECDSA");
  1122	   203	            signer.Init(false, publicKey);
  1123	   204	            signer.BlockUpdate(hash, 0, hash.Length);
  1124	   205	            signer.VerifySignature(derSignature).Should().BeTrue();
  1125	   206	        }
  1126	   207	
  1127	   208	        private static (BigInteger R, BigInteger S) ParseDerEcdsa(byte[] der)
  1128	   209	        {
  1129	   210	            using var ms = new MemoryStream(der);
  1130	   211	            using var reader = new Asn1InputStream(ms);
  1131	   212	            var seq = (DerSequence)reader.ReadObject();
  1132	   213	            var r = ((DerInteger)seq[0]).Value;
  1133	   214	            var s = ((DerInteger)seq[1]).Value;
  1134	   215	            return (r, s);
  1135	   216	        }
  1136	   217	    }
  1137	   218	}

  1400	src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

  2694	codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:651:src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

  3145	codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5069:- Raw JWT `r||s` signatures are converted to DER only because BouncyCastle verifies DER; the verified mathematical signature is the same.
  3146	codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5083:| C# file:line | TS upstream path:line | Semantic diff | Suggested fix |
  3147	codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5087:| `src/Crypto.cs:435-438` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:95-151` | `HpkeEncrypt` treats null `PlainTextBuf` as empty and does not wrap errors as `Unable to perform hpkeEncrypt`. TS catches and wraps all errors. | Require non-null plaintext and wrap exceptions with the upstream message. |
  3148	codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5088:| `src/Crypto.cs:386-421` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:308-350` | `HpkeDecrypt` throws C# argument/crypto exceptions directly; TS wraps all errors as `Unable to perform hpkeDecrypt: ...`. | Wrap non-fatal exceptions with upstream-equivalent message. |
  3149	codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5090:| `src/Crypto.cs:506` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:426-462` | C# only supports P-256; upstream exposes optional `curve` and supports `CURVE_SECP256K1`. | Add curve parameter/support or document intentional P-256-only subset. |
  3150	codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5091:| `src/Crypto.cs:509-512` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:430-432` | Invalid compressed length error message differs. | Use upstream message if exact error parity matters. |
  3151	codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5092:| `src/Crypto.cs:579-582` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:286-299` | C# rejects buffers `<= 33`; TS slices first 33 bytes and allows downstream behavior. A valid compressed key plus empty ciphertext would stringify upstream but throw in C#. | Match TS slicing behavior, or only reject `< 33` if defensive validation is desired. |
  3152	codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5093:| `src/Crypto.cs:614-619` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:76-77` | C# falls back from Base58Check to raw Base58; upstream only accepts Base58Check. This can accept bundles TS rejects. | Remove raw Base58 fallback. |
  3153	codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5095:| `src/Crypto.cs:665-708` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:325-360` | `EncryptPrivateKeyToBundle` verifies/parses bundle before `DecodeKey`; upstream decodes private key before signature verification. Error precedence differs when both inputs are bad. | Move `DecodeKey` immediately after JSON parse to match upstream. |
  3154	codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5096:| `src/Crypto.cs:753-767`, `1052` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:124-162` | C# accepts a legacy `signature`/`signedData` envelope path not present in upstream 2.8.8. | Remove from this port or isolate as a separately named compatibility helper. |
  3155	codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5097:| `src/Crypto.cs:796-803` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:144-151` | C# allows missing `organizationId`; upstream rejects missing or mismatched `organizationId`. | Require `bundleOrgId` to be non-empty and equal. |
  3156	codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5098:| `src/Crypto.cs:827-830` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:164-180` | SOLANA export output is wrong: C# Base58-encodes only 32 decrypted private-key bytes; upstream derives Ed25519 public key, concatenates 32 private + 32 public bytes, then Base58-encodes 64 bytes. | Derive Ed25519 public key and encode the 64-byte concatenation. |
  3157	codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5099:| `src/Crypto.cs:853-890` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:452-477` | Malformed JWT behavior differs: C# returns `false` for wrong part count and catches all errors; upstream throws for missing signature part and returns verifier result otherwise. | Throw on malformed JWT and avoid catch-all if exact upstream behavior is required. |
  3158	codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5105:`tests/Fixtures/` currently contains only `tests/Fixtures/encoding/turnkey-encoding-vectors.json`; no fixture under `tests/Fixtures/` exercises `src/Crypto.cs`. `tests/Fixtures/README.md` lists `crypto/` as planned, but the directory is absent. `tests/CryptoTests.cs` uses inline RFC/NIST/random roundtrip tests and explicitly states positive Turnkey-signed bundle tests are not present.

  3177	codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5196:| `src/Crypto.cs:827-830` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:164-180` | SOLANA export output is wrong: C# Base58-encodes only 32 decrypted private-key bytes; upstream derives Ed25519 public key, concatenates 32 private + 32 public bytes, then Base58-encodes 64 bytes. | Derive Ed25519 public key and encode the 64-byte concatenation. |
  3178	codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5197:| `src/Crypto.cs:853-890` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:452-477` | Malformed JWT behavior differs: C# returns `false` for wrong part count and catches all errors; upstream throws for missing signature part and returns verifier result otherwise. | Throw on malformed JWT and avoid catch-all if exact upstream behavior is required. |
  3179	codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5203:`tests/Fixtures/` currently contains only `tests/Fixtures/encoding/turnkey-encoding-vectors.json`; no fixture under `tests/Fixtures/` exercises `src/Crypto.cs`. `tests/Fixtures/README.md` lists `crypto/` as planned, but the directory is absent. `tests/CryptoTests.cs` uses inline RFC/NIST/random roundtrip tests and explicitly states positive Turnkey-signed bundle tests are not present.
  3180	codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5205:So I cannot confirm that every Crypto.cs fixture was generated from the pinned upstream package or that C# asserts the same bytes Node would produce. There are no applicable Crypto fixtures to compare.

  5345	   339	        /// <summary>

  5349	   343	        /// <remarks>
  5350	   344	        /// Upstream uses a pure-JS <c>btoa</c> for React Native compatibility and
  5351	   345	        /// throws on code points greater than 0xFF. This port preserves that
  5352	   346	        /// behavior by checking each code point against 0xFF before delegating to
  5353	   347	        /// <c>System.Convert.ToBase64String</c>, which is wire-identical for the
  5354	   348	        /// 0-255 byte range.
  5355	   349	        /// </remarks>
  5356	   350	        public static string StringToBase64UrlString(string input)
  5357	   351	        {
  5358	   352	            if (input == null)
  5359	   353	            {
  5360	   354	                throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(input));
  5361	   355	            }

  5363	   357	            var bytes = new byte[input.Length];
  5364	   358	            for (int i = 0; i < input.Length; i++)
  5365	   359	            {
  5366	   360	                int cp = input[i];
  5367	   361	                if (cp > 0xFF)
  5368	   362	                {
  5369	   363	                    throw new ArgumentException(
  5370	   364	                        "InvalidCharacterError: found code point greater than 255:"
  5371	   365	                        + cp + " at position " + i);
  5372	   366	                }
  5373	   367	                bytes[i] = (byte)cp;
  5374	   368	            }
  5375	   369	
  5376	   370	            return Base64StringToBase64UrlEncodedString(Convert.ToBase64String(bytes));

  5859	   101	- "review", "code review", "review code", "codex 审核"
  5860	   102	- "帮我审核", "检查代码", "审一下", "看看代码"
  5861	   103	
  5862	   104	## Core Concept: Intention vs Implementation
  5863	   105	
  5864	   106	Running `codex review --uncommitted` alone only shows AI "what was done (Implementation)".
  5865	   107	Recording intention first tells AI "what you wanted to do (Intention)".
  5866	   108	
  5867	   109	**"Code changes + intention description" as combined input is the most effective way to improve AI code review quality.**
  5868	   110	
  5869	   111	## Skill Architecture
  5870	   112	
  5871	   113	This skill operates in two phases:
  5872	   114	
  5873	   115	1. **Preparation Phase** (current context): Check working directory, update CHANGELOG
  5874	   116	2. **Review Phase** (isolated context): Invoke Task tool to execute Lint + codex review (using context: fork to reduce context waste)
  5875	   117	
  5876	   118	## Execution Steps
  5877	   119	
  5878	   120	### 0. [First] Check Working Directory Status
  5879	   121	
  5880	   122	```bash
  5881	   123	git diff --name-only && git status --short
  5882	   124	```
  5883	   125	
  5884	   126	**Decide review mode based on output:**
  5885	   127	
  5886	   128	- **Has uncommitted changes** → Continue with steps 1-4 (normal flow)
  5887	   129	- **Clean working directory** → Directly invoke codex-runner: `codex review --commit HEAD`
  5888	   130	
  5889	   131	### 1. [Mandatory] Check if CHANGELOG is Updated
  5890	   132	
  5891	   133	**Before any review, must check if CHANGELOG.md contains description of current changes.**
  5892	   134	
  5893	   135	```bash
  5894	   136	# Check if CHANGELOG.md is in uncommitted changes
  5895	   137	git diff --name-only | grep -E "(CHANGELOG|changelog)"
  5896	   138	```
  5897	   139	
  5898	   140	**If CHANGELOG is not updated, you must automatically perform the following (don't ask user to do it manually):**
  5899	   141	
  5900	   142	1. **Analyze changes**: Run `git diff --stat` and `git diff` to get complete changes
  5901	   143	2. **Auto-generate CHANGELOG entry**: Generate compliant entry based on code changes
  5902	   144	3. **Write to CHANGELOG.md**: Use Edit tool to insert entry at top of `[Unreleased]` section
  5903	   145	4. **Continue review flow**: Immediately proceed to next steps after CHANGELOG update
  5904	   146	
  5905	   147	**Auto-generated CHANGELOG entry format:**
  5906	   148	
  5907	   149	```markdown
  5908	   150	## [Unreleased]
  5909	   151	
  5910	   152	### Added / Changed / Fixed
  5911	   153	
  5912	   154	- Feature description: what problem was solved or what functionality was implemented
  5913	   155	- Affected files: main modified files/modules
  5914	   156	```
  5915	   157	
  5916	   158	**Example - Auto-generation Flow:**
  5917	   159	
  5918	   160	```
  5919	   161	1. Detected CHANGELOG not updated
  5920	   162	2. Run git diff --stat, found handlers/responses.go modified (+88 lines)
  5921	   163	3. Run git diff to analyze details: added CompactHandler function
  5922	   164	4. Auto-generate entry:
  5923	   165	   ### Added
  5924	   166	   - Added `/v1/responses/compact` endpoint for conversation context compression
  5925	   167	   - Supports multi-channel failover and request body size limits
  5926	   168	5. Use Edit tool to write to CHANGELOG.md
  5927	   169	6. Continue with lint and codex review
  5928	   170	```
  5929	   171	
  5930	   172	### 2. [Critical] Stage All New Files
  5931	   173	
  5932	   174	**Before invoking codex review, must add all new files (untracked files) to git staging area, otherwise codex will report P1 error.**
  5933	   175	
  5934	   176	```bash
  5935	   177	# Check for new files
  5936	   178	git status --short | grep "^??"
  5937	   179	```
  5938	   180	
  5939	   181	**If there are new files, automatically execute:**
  5940	   182	
  5941	   183	```bash
  5942	   184	# Safely stage all new files (handles empty list and special filenames)
  5943	   185	git ls-files --others --exclude-standard -z | while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do git add -- "$f"; done
  5944	   186	```
  5945	   187	
  5946	   188	**Explanation:**
  5947	   189	
  5948	   190	- `-z` uses null character to separate filenames, correctly handles filenames with spaces/newlines
  5949	   191	- `while IFS= read -r -d ''` reads filenames one by one
  5950	   192	- `git add -- "$f"` uses `--` separator, correctly handles filenames starting with `-`
  5951	   193	- When no new files exist, loop body doesn't execute, safely skipped
  5952	   194	- This won't stage modified files, only handles new files
  5953	   195	- codex needs files to be tracked by git for proper review
  5954	   196	
  5955	   197	### 3. Evaluate Task Difficulty and Invoke codex-runner
  5956	   198	
  5957	   199	**Count change scale:**
  5958	   200	
  5959	   201	```bash
  5960	   202	# Count number of changed files and lines of code
  5961	   203	git diff --stat | tail -1
  5962	   204	```
  5963	   205	
  5964	   206	**Difficulty Assessment Criteria:**
  5965	   207	
  5966	   208	**Difficult Tasks** (meets any condition):
  5967	   209	
  5968	   210	- Modified files ≥ 10
  5969	   211	- Total code changes (insertions + deletions) ≥ 500 lines
  5970	   212	- Single metric: insertions ≥ 300 lines OR deletions ≥ 300 lines
  5971	   213	- Involves core architecture/algorithm changes
  5972	   214	- Cross-module refactoring
  5973	   215	- Config: `model_reasoning_effort=xhigh`, timeout 30 minutes
  5974	   216	
  5975	   217	**Normal Tasks** (other cases):
  5976	   218	
  5977	   219	- Config: `model_reasoning_effort=high`, timeout 10 minutes
  5978	   220

  6048	
  6049	**B. Method Coverage Table**
  6050	
  6051	| C# method (file:line) | Upstream TS function (path:line) | Status | Notes |
  6052	|---|---|---|---|
  6053	| `ApiKeyStamper(string apiPublicKey, string apiPrivateKey)` (`src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:62`) | `ApiKeyStamper.constructor(config)` (`ts-source/index.ts:78`) | REVIEWED | Stores key material, but C# also validates private key at construction; see E. |
  6054	| `Stamp(string payload)` (`src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:118`) | `ApiKeyStamper.stamp(payload)` (`ts-source/index.ts:84`) | REVIEWED | JSON stamp field names/order and `X-Stamp` value construction reviewed. |
  6055	| `SignWithApiKey(string content)` (`src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:148`) | `purejs.signWithApiKey(input)` (`ts-source/purejs.ts:5`) | REVIEWED | Mirrors purejs path: derive compressed pubkey, compare, SHA-256, P-256 ECDSA, low-S, DER hex. |
  6056	| `EncodeDerSignatureHex(BigInteger r, BigInteger s)` (`src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:199`) | `signature.toDERHex()` (`ts-source/purejs.ts:23`) | REVIEWED | Upstream DER implementation is in `@noble/curves`, not local TS; local DER positive INTEGER encoding reviewed against expected DER form. |
  6057	
  6058	**C. Intentional Adaptations**
  6059	
  6060	- TS config object constructor -> C# positional constructor: language/API-shape adaptation; same key values are stored.
  6061	- TS `async`/dynamic import -> synchronous C# calls: no JS module runtime in C#; output is immediate equivalent for the reviewed purejs path.
  6062	- TS object literal stamp -> C# DTO with `JsonPropertyName`: preserves `publicKey`, `scheme`, `signature` names and declaration order.
  6063	- `JSON.stringify` -> source-generated `System.Text.Json`: structural AOT adaptation; valid stamp fields are ASCII hex/constants, so emitted JSON bytes match expected field names/order.
  6064	- `@turnkey/encoding` helpers -> local `Encoding` helpers: structural port of hex/base64url helpers used for the stamp header.
  6065	- `@noble/curves` P-256 -> BouncyCastle P-256 primitives: implementation substitution for scalar multiplication and ECDSA while preserving P-256/SHA-256/DER output intent.
  6066	- Noble `toDERHex()` -> local DER writer: structural local helper for the same ASN.1 SEQUENCE of positive INTEGER `r` and `s`.
  6067	
  6068	**D. D17 Enforcement Check**
  6069	
  6070	N/A (not Crypto.cs).
  6071	
  6072	**E. Logic Divergence Findings**
  6073	
  6074	| C# file:line | TS upstream path:line | Semantic diff | Suggested fix |
  6075	|---|---|---|---|
  6076	| `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:148` and comment scope at `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:13` | `ts-source/index.ts:31`, `ts-source/index.ts:56`, `ts-source/index.ts:84` | C# always follows the purejs-style deterministic RFC6979 path. Upstream `ApiKeyStamper.stamp()` defaults through `detectRuntime()`, so in Node it dispatches to `nodecrypto.ts`, whose ECDSA signature bytes are not expected to be deterministic. Repeated C# stamps can therefore differ from upstream default Node stamps even for the same key/payload. | Make the parity target explicit in tests and docs as `runtimeOverride = "purejs"` parity, or change the C# implementation/fixtures to match the selected upstream default behavior. |
  6077	| `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:62` | `ts-source/index.ts:78`, `ts-source/purejs.ts:10` | C# validates/parses the private key in the constructor and throws before any signing call. Upstream constructor only assigns fields; invalid key material is rejected later when signing derives/imports the key. This changes error timing and observable construction behavior. | Defer private-key parsing/range validation until `SignWithApiKey`, or explicitly document constructor-time validation as an intentional behavior change. |
  6078

  6082

   517	    38	namespace Turnkey

   519	    40	    /// <summary>
   520	    41	    /// Stamper for Turnkey API requests. 1:1 logical port of upstream
   521	    42	    /// <c>@turnkey/api-key-stamper</c> at peak's pinned version 0.5.0.
   522	    43	    /// </summary>
   523	    44	    /// <remarks>
   524	    45	    /// <para><b>Runtime parity target:</b> this port mirrors upstream's
   525	    46	    /// <c>purejs</c> runtime (deterministic ECDSA via RFC 6979 + low-S).
   526	    47	    /// Upstream's default <c>detectRuntime()</c> picks <c>"node"</c> in
   527	    48	    /// Node which dispatches to <c>nodecrypto.ts</c> and produces
   528	    49	    /// non-deterministic signatures (random k); behavior parity with
   529	    50	    /// that runtime is intentionally NOT a goal — Turnkey's backend
   530	    51	    /// accepts either, but the C# wire bytes will only match
   531	    52	    /// deterministically against upstream <c>runtimeOverride: "purejs"</c>
   532	    53	    /// callers. All three runtimes produce signatures that verify against
   533	    54	    /// the same public key, so end-to-end Turnkey API calls work
   534	    55	    /// regardless.</para>
   535	    56	    /// <para><b>Validation timing:</b> the constructor parses but does NOT

   540	    61	    public class ApiKeyStamper

   567	    88	        /// <summary>

   622	   143	        /// <summary>
   623	   144	        /// Mirror of upstream <c>purejs.ts signWithApiKey</c>. Verifies that
   624	   145	        /// the configured public key matches the one derived from the
   625	   146	        /// configured private key, then SHA-256-hashes the payload and signs

   633	   154	            // Upstream `purejs.ts`:

   635	   156	            //   const publicKeyString = uint8ArrayToHexString(publicKey);

   637	   158	            //
   638	   159	            // Crypto.GetPublicKey enforces the noble invariants (exact 32-byte

   640	   161	            // key. Per the upstream pattern this validation happens at sign

   645	   166	            {

   647	   168	                    "Bad API key. Expected to get public key " + _apiPublicKey
   648	   169	                    + ", got " + derivedPublicKey);
   649	   170	            }
   650	   171	
   651	   172	            // Upstream:
   652	   173	            //   const hash = createHash().update(input.content).digest();   // SHA-256
   653	   174	            //   const signature = p256.sign(hash, input.privateKey);        // RFC 6979 + lowS
   654	   175	            //   return signature.toDERHex();
   655	   176	            var curve = ECNamedCurveTable.GetByName(CryptoConstants.CURVE_NAME);
   656	   177	            var domainParams = new ECDomainParameters(curve.Curve, curve.G, curve.N, curve.H, curve.GetSeed());
   657	   178	            var privateKeyBytes = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(_apiPrivateKey);
   658	   179	            var privateKeyScalar = new BigInteger(1, privateKeyBytes);
   659	   180	
   660	   181	            byte[] payloadBytes = System.Text.Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(content);
   661	   182	
   662	   183	            var digest = DigestUtilities.GetDigest("SHA-256");
   663	   184	            digest.BlockUpdate(payloadBytes, 0, payloadBytes.Length);
   664	   185	            var hash = new byte[digest.GetDigestSize()];
   665	   186	            digest.DoFinal(hash, 0);
   666	   187	
   667	   188	            var hmacCalculator = new HMacDsaKCalculator(DigestUtilities.GetDigest("SHA-256"));
   668	   189	            var signer = new ECDsaSigner(hmacCalculator);
   669	   190	            signer.Init(true, new ECPrivateKeyParameters(privateKeyScalar, domainParams));
   670	   191	
   671	   192	            BigInteger[] signature = signer.GenerateSignature(hash);
   672	   193	            BigInteger r = signature[0];
   673	   194	            BigInteger s = signature[1];
   674	   195	
   675	   196	            // Low-S normalization (noble enables lowS by default).
   676	   197	            BigInteger halfN = domainParams.N.ShiftRight(1);
   677	   198	            if (s.CompareTo(halfN) > 0)
   678	   199	            {
   679	   200	                s = domainParams.N.Subtract(s);
   680	   201	            }
   681	   202	
   682	   203	            return EncodeDerSignatureHex(r, s);
   683	   204	        }
   684	   205	
   685	   206	        /// <summary>
   686	   207	        /// Encode the <c>(r, s)</c> pair as a DER ASN.1 SEQUENCE of two
   687	   208	        /// INTEGERs, then hex-encode the result.
   688	   209	        /// Equivalent to noble's <c>signature.toDERHex()</c>.
   689	   210	        /// </summary>
   690	   211	        private static string EncodeDerSignatureHex(BigInteger r, BigInteger s)
   691	   212	        {
   692	   213	            byte[] rBytes = r.ToByteArrayUnsigned();
   693	   214	            byte[] sBytes = s.ToByteArrayUnsigned();
   694	   215	
   695	   216	            bool rNeedsPadding = rBytes.Length > 0 && (rBytes[0] & 0x80) != 0;
   696	   217	            bool sNeedsPadding = sBytes.Length > 0 && (sBytes[0] & 0x80) != 0;
   697	   218	            int rLen = rBytes.Length + (rNeedsPadding ? 1 : 0);
   698	   219	            int sLen = sBytes.Length + (sNeedsPadding ? 1 : 0);
   699	   220	
   700	   221	            int contentLen = 2 + rLen + 2 + sLen; // (INTEGER tag + length) * 2 + r + s
   701	   222	
   702	   223	            using (var ms = new MemoryStream())
   703	   224	            {
   704	   225	                ms.WriteByte(0x30); // SEQUENCE
   705	   226	
   706	   227	                if (contentLen >= 128)
   707	   228	                {
   708	   229	                    // Long-form length (1 octet count). For ECDSA P-256
   709	   230	                    // signatures the total length never exceeds 0x7f in
   710	   231	                    // practice; this branch is defensive.
   711	   232	                    ms.WriteByte(0x81);
   712	   233	                    ms.WriteByte((byte)contentLen);
   713	   234	                }
   714	   235	                else
   715	   236	                {
   716	   237	                    ms.WriteByte((byte)contentLen);
   717	   238	                }
   718	   239	
   719	   240	                ms.WriteByte(0x02); // INTEGER tag (r)
   720	   241	                ms.WriteByte((byte)rLen);
   721	   242	                if (rNeedsPadding) ms.WriteByte(0x00);
   722	   243	                ms.Write(rBytes, 0, rBytes.Length);
   723	   244	
   724	   245	                ms.WriteByte(0x02); // INTEGER tag (s)
   725	   246	                ms.WriteByte((byte)sLen);
   726	   247	                if (sNeedsPadding) ms.WriteByte(0x00);
   727	   248	                ms.Write(sBytes, 0, sBytes.Length);
   728	   249	
   729	   250	                return Encoding.Uint8ArrayToHexString(ms.ToArray());
   730	   251	            }
   731	   252	        }
   732	   253	    }

   738	     1	/// <reference lib="dom" />
   739	     2	import { stringToBase64urlString } from "@turnkey/encoding";
   740	     3	
   741	     4	// Header name for an API key stamp
   742	     5	const stampHeaderName = "X-Stamp";
   743	     6	
   744	     7	export type Runtime = "browser" | "node" | "purejs";
   745	     8	
   746	     9	export type TApiKeyStamperConfig = {
   747	    10	  apiPublicKey: string;
   748	    11	  apiPrivateKey: string;
   749	    12	  runtimeOverride?: Runtime | undefined;
   750	    13	};
   751	    14	
   752	    15	// `window.document` ensures that we're in a browser context
   753	    16	// and `crypto.subtle` ensures that it supports the web crypto APIs
   754	    17	// Inspired by https://github.com/flexdinesh/browser-or-node/blob/master/src/index.ts
   755	    18	const isCryptoEnabledBrowser: boolean =
   756	    19	  typeof window !== "undefined" &&
   757	    20	  typeof window.document !== "undefined" &&
   758	    21	  typeof crypto !== "undefined" &&
   759	    22	  typeof crypto.subtle !== "undefined";
   760	    23	
   761	    24	// We check `process.versions.node`
   762	    25	// Taken from https://github.com/flexdinesh/browser-or-node/blob/master/src/index.ts
   763	    26	const isNode: boolean =
   764	    27	  typeof process !== "undefined" &&
   765	    28	  process.versions != null &&
   766	    29	  process.versions.node != null;
   767	    30	
   768	    31	const detectRuntime = (): Runtime => {
   769	    32	  if (isCryptoEnabledBrowser) {
   770	    33	    return "browser";
   771	    34	  }
   772	    35	
   773	    36	  if (isNode) {
   774	    37	    return "node";
   775	    38	  }
   776	    39	
   777	    40	  // If we don't have NodeJS or web crypto at our disposal, default to pure JS implementation
   778	    41	  // This is the case for old browsers and react native environments
   779	    42	  return "purejs";
   780	    43	};
   781	    44	
   782	    45	/**
   783	    46	 * Signature function abstracting the differences between NodeJS and web environments for signing with API keys.
   784	    47	 */
   785	    48	export const signWithApiKey = async (
   786	    49	  input: {
   787	    50	    content: string;
   788	    51	    publicKey: string;
   789	    52	    privateKey: string;
   790	    53	  },
   791	    54	  runtimeOverride?: Runtime | undefined,
   792	    55	): Promise<string> => {
   793	    56	  const runtime = runtimeOverride ?? detectRuntime();
   794	    57	
   795	    58	  switch (runtime) {
   796	    59	    case "browser":
   797	    60	      return (await import("./webcrypto")).signWithApiKey(input);
   798	    61	    case "node":
   799	    62	      return (await import("./nodecrypto")).signWithApiKey(input);
   800	    63	    case "purejs":
   801	    64	      return (await import("./purejs")).signWithApiKey(input);
   802	    65	    default:
   803	    66	      throw new Error(`Unsupported runtime: ${runtime}`);
   804	    67	  }
   805	    68	};
   806	    69	
   807	    70	/**
   808	    71	 * Stamper to use with `@turnkey/http`'s `TurnkeyClient`
   809	    72	 */
   810	    73	export class ApiKeyStamper {
   811	    74	  apiPublicKey: string;
   812	    75	  apiPrivateKey: string;
   813	    76	  runtimeOverride?: Runtime | undefined;
   814	    77	
   815	    78	  constructor(config: TApiKeyStamperConfig) {
   816	    79	    this.apiPublicKey = config.apiPublicKey;
   817	    80	    this.apiPrivateKey = config.apiPrivateKey;
   818	    81	    this.runtimeOverride = config.runtimeOverride;
   819	    82	  }
   820	    83	
   821	    84	  async stamp(payload: string) {
   822	    85	    const signature = await signWithApiKey(
   823	    86	      {
   824	    87	        publicKey: this.apiPublicKey,
   825	    88	        privateKey: this.apiPrivateKey,
   826	    89	        content: payload,
   827	    90	      },
   828	    91	      this.runtimeOverride,
   829	    92	    );
   830	    93	
   831	    94	    const stamp = {
   832	    95	      publicKey: this.apiPublicKey,
   833	    96	      scheme: "SIGNATURE_SCHEME_TK_API_P256",
   834	    97	      signature,
   835	    98	    };
   836	    99	
   837	   100	    return {
   838	   101	      stampHeaderName,
   839	   102	      stampHeaderValue: stringToBase64urlString(JSON.stringify(stamp)),
   840	   103	    };
   841	   104	  }
   842	   105	}
   843	   106	
   844	   107	export { pointDecode } from "./tink/elliptic_curves";
   845	     1	import { p256 } from "@noble/curves/p256";
   846	     2	import { createHash } from "sha256-uint8array";
   847	     3	import { uint8ArrayToHexString } from "@turnkey/encoding";
   848	     4	
   849	     5	export const signWithApiKey = async (input: {
   850	     6	  content: string;
   851	     7	  publicKey: string;
   852	     8	  privateKey: string;
   853	     9	}): Promise<string> => {
   854	    10	  const publicKey = p256.getPublicKey(input.privateKey, true);
   855	    11	
   856	    12	  // Public key in the usual 02 or 03 + 64 hex digits
   857	    13	  const publicKeyString = uint8ArrayToHexString(publicKey);
   858	    14	
   859	    15	  if (publicKeyString != input.publicKey) {
   860	    16	    throw new Error(
   861	    17	      `Bad API key. Expected to get public key ${input.publicKey}, got ${publicKeyString}`,
   862	    18	    );
   863	    19	  }
   864	    20	
   865	    21	  const hash = createHash().update(input.content).digest();
   866	    22	  const signature = p256.sign(hash, input.privateKey);
   867	    23	  return signature.toDERHex();
   868	    24	};
   869	     1	import { pointDecode } from "./tink/elliptic_curves";
   870	     2	import {
   871	     3	  hexStringToBase64url,
   872	     4	  uint8ArrayFromHexString,
   873	     5	  DEFAULT_JWK_MEMBER_BYTE_LENGTH,
   874	     6	} from "@turnkey/encoding";
   875	     7	
   876	     8	/**
   877	     9	 * Converts a Turnkey API key pair into a JSON Web Key (JWK) format.
   878	    10	 * This function accepts P-256 API keys only.
   879	    11	 *
   880	    12	 * @param {Object} input - The Turnkey API key components.
   881	    13	 * @param {string} input.uncompressedPrivateKeyHex - Hexadecimal-encoded uncompressed private key (32-byte scalar).
   882	    14	 * @param {string} input.compressedPublicKeyHex - Hexadecimal-encoded compressed public key (33 bytes).
   883	    15	 * @returns {JsonWebKey} A JSON Web Key object representing the EC P-256 key.
   884	    16	 */
   885	    17	export function convertTurnkeyApiKeyToJwk(input: {
   886	    18	  uncompressedPrivateKeyHex: string;
   887	    19	  compressedPublicKeyHex: string;
   888	    20	}): JsonWebKey {
   889	    21	  const { uncompressedPrivateKeyHex, compressedPublicKeyHex } = input;
   890	    22	
   891	    23	  let jwk;
   892	    24	  try {
   893	    25	    jwk = pointDecode(uint8ArrayFromHexString(compressedPublicKeyHex));
   894	    26	  } catch (e) {
   895	    27	    throw new Error(
   896	    28	      `unable to load API key: invalid public key. Did you switch your public and private key by accident? Is your public key a valid, compressed P-256 public key?`,
   897	    29	    );
   898	    30	  }
   899	    31	
   900	    32	  // Ensure that d is sufficiently padded
   901	    33	  jwk.d = hexStringToBase64url(
   902	    34	    uncompressedPrivateKeyHex,
   903	    35	    DEFAULT_JWK_MEMBER_BYTE_LENGTH,
   904	    36	  );
   905	    37	
   906	    38	  return jwk;
   907	    39	}
   908	     1	import * as crypto from "crypto";
   909	     2	import { convertTurnkeyApiKeyToJwk } from "./utils";
   910	     3	
   911	     4	export const signWithApiKey = async (input: {
   912	     5	  content: string;
   913	     6	  publicKey: string;
   914	     7	  privateKey: string;
   915	     8	}): Promise<string> => {
   916	     9	  const { content, publicKey, privateKey } = input;
   917	    10	
   918	    11	  const privateKeyObject = crypto.createPrivateKey({
   919	    12	    // @ts-expect-error -- the key can be a JWK object since Node v15.12.0
   920	    13	    // https://nodejs.org/api/crypto.html#cryptocreateprivatekeykey
   921	    14	    key: convertTurnkeyApiKeyToJwk({
   922	    15	      uncompressedPrivateKeyHex: privateKey,
   923	    16	      compressedPublicKeyHex: publicKey,
   924	    17	    }),
   925	    18	    format: "jwk",
   926	    19	  });
   927	    20	
   928	    21	  const sign = crypto.createSign("SHA256");
   929	    22	  sign.write(Buffer.from(content));
   930	    23	  sign.end();
   931	    24	
   932	    25	  return sign.sign(privateKeyObject, "hex");
   933	    26	};
   934	     1	/// <reference lib="dom" />
   935	     2	import { convertTurnkeyApiKeyToJwk } from "./utils";
   936	     3	import { uint8ArrayToHexString } from "@turnkey/encoding";
   937	     4	
   938	     5	export const signWithApiKey = async (input: {
   939	     6	  content: string;
   940	     7	  publicKey: string;
   941	     8	  privateKey: string;
   942	     9	}): Promise<string> => {
   943	    10	  const { content, publicKey, privateKey } = input;
   944	    11	
   945	    12	  const key = await importTurnkeyApiKey({
   946	    13	    uncompressedPrivateKeyHex: privateKey,
   947	    14	    compressedPublicKeyHex: publicKey,
   948	    15	  });
   949	    16	  return await signMessage({ key, content });
   950	    17	};
   951	    18	
   952	    19	/**
   953	    20	 * Imports a P-256 Turnkey API key into a WebCrypto `CryptoKey`.
   954	    21	 *
   955	    22	 * @param {Object} input - The Turnkey API key components.
   956	    23	 * @param {string} input.uncompressedPrivateKeyHex - Hexadecimal-encoded uncompressed private key (32-byte scalar).
   957	    24	 * @param {string} input.compressedPublicKeyHex - Hexadecimal-encoded compressed public key (33 bytes).
   958	    25	 * @returns {Promise<CryptoKey>} A `CryptoKey` object representing a P-256 key.
   959	    26	 */
   960	    27	async function importTurnkeyApiKey(input: {
   961	    28	  uncompressedPrivateKeyHex: string;
   962	    29	  compressedPublicKeyHex: string;
   963	    30	}): Promise<CryptoKey> {
   964	    31	  const { uncompressedPrivateKeyHex, compressedPublicKeyHex } = input;
   965	    32	
   966	    33	  const jwk = convertTurnkeyApiKeyToJwk({
   967	    34	    uncompressedPrivateKeyHex,
   968	    35	    compressedPublicKeyHex,
   969	    36	  });
   970	    37	
   971	    38	  return await crypto.subtle.importKey(
   972	    39	    "jwk",
   973	    40	    jwk,
   974	    41	    {
   975	    42	      name: "ECDSA",
   976	    43	      namedCurve: "P-256",
   977	    44	    },
   978	    45	    false, // not extractable
   979	    46	    ["sign"], // allow signing
   980	    47	  );
   981	    48	}
   982	    49	
   983	    50	async function signMessage(input: {
   984	    51	  key: CryptoKey;
   985	    52	  content: string;
   986	    53	}): Promise<string> {
   987	    54	  const { key, content } = input;
   988	    55	
   989	    56	  const signatureIeee1363 = await crypto.subtle.sign(
   990	    57	    {
   991	    58	      name: "ECDSA",
   992	    59	      hash: "SHA-256",
   993	    60	    },
   994	    61	    key,
   995	    62	    new TextEncoder().encode(content),
   996	    63	  );
   997	    64	
   998	    65	  const signatureDer = convertEcdsaIeee1363ToDer(
   999	    66	    new Uint8Array(signatureIeee1363),
  1000	    67	  );
  1001	    68	
  1002	    69	  return uint8ArrayToHexString(signatureDer);
  1003	    70	}
  1004	    71	
  1005	    72	/**
  1006	    73	 * `SubtleCrypto.sign(...)` outputs signature in IEEE P1363 format:
  1007	    74	 * - https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/SubtleCrypto/sign#ecdsa
  1008	    75	 *
  1009	    76	 * Turnkey expects the signature encoding to be DER-encoded ASN.1:
  1010	    77	 * - https://github.com/tkhq/tkcli/blob/7f0159af5a73387ff050647180d1db4d3a3aa033/src/internal/apikey/apikey.go#L149
  1011	    78	 *
  1012	    79	 * Code modified from https://github.com/google/tink/blob/6f74b99a2bfe6677e3670799116a57268fd067fa/javascript/subtle/elliptic_curves.ts#L114
  1013	    80	 *
  1014	    81	 * Transform an ECDSA signature in IEEE 1363 encoding to DER encoding.
  1015	    82	 *
  1016	    83	 * @param ieee the ECDSA signature in IEEE encoding
  1017	    84	 * @return ECDSA signature in DER encoding
  1018	    85	 */
  1019	    86	function convertEcdsaIeee1363ToDer(ieee: Uint8Array): Uint8Array {
  1020	    87	  if (ieee.length % 2 != 0 || ieee.length == 0 || ieee.length > 132) {
  1021	    88	    throw new Error(
  1022	    89	      "Invalid IEEE P1363 signature encoding. Length: " + ieee.length,
  1023	    90	    );
  1024	    91	  }
  1025	    92	  const r = toUnsignedBigNum(ieee.subarray(0, ieee.length / 2));
  1026	    93	  const s = toUnsignedBigNum(ieee.subarray(ieee.length / 2, ieee.length));
  1027	    94	  let offset = 0;
  1028	    95	  const length = 1 + 1 + r.length + 1 + 1 + s.length;
  1029	    96	  let der;
  1030	    97	  if (length >= 128) {
  1031	    98	    der = new Uint8Array(length + 3);
  1032	    99	    der[offset++] = 48;
  1033	   100	    der[offset++] = 128 + 1;
  1034	   101	    der[offset++] = length;
  1035	   102	  } else {
  1036	   103	    der = new Uint8Array(length + 2);
  1037	   104	    der[offset++] = 48;
  1038	   105	    der[offset++] = length;
  1039	   106	  }
  1040	   107	  der[offset++] = 2;
  1041	   108	  der[offset++] = r.length;
  1042	   109	  der.set(r, offset);
  1043	   110	  offset += r.length;
  1044	   111	  der[offset++] = 2;
  1045	   112	  der[offset++] = s.length;
  1046	   113	  der.set(s, offset);
  1047	   114	  return der;
  1048	   115	}
  1049	   116	
  1050	   117	/**
  1051	   118	 * Code modified from https://github.com/google/tink/blob/6f74b99a2bfe6677e3670799116a57268fd067fa/javascript/subtle/elliptic_curves.ts#L311
  1052	   119	 *
  1053	   120	 * Transform a big integer in big endian to minimal unsigned form which has
  1054	   121	 * no extra zero at the beginning except when the highest bit is set.
  1055	   122	 */
  1056	   123	function toUnsignedBigNum(bytes: Uint8Array): Uint8Array {
  1057	   124	  // Remove zero prefixes.
  1058	   125	  let start = 0;
  1059	   126	  while (start < bytes.length && bytes[start] == 0) {
  1060	   127	    start++;
  1061	   128	  }
  1062	   129	  if (start == bytes.length) {
  1063	   130	    start = bytes.length - 1;
  1064	   131	  }
  1065	   132	  let extraZero = 0;
  1066	   133	
  1067	   134	  // If the 1st bit is not zero, add 1 zero byte.
  1068	   135	  if ((bytes[start]! & 128) == 128) {
  1069	   136	    // Add extra zero.
  1070	   137	    extraZero = 1;
  1071	   138	  }
  1072	   139	  const res = new Uint8Array(bytes.length - start + extraZero);
  1073	   140	  res.set(bytes.subarray(start), extraZero);
  1074	   141	  return res;
  1075	   142	}

  1080	     1	// ApiKeyStamperTests.cs — xunit tests for src/ApiKeyStamper.cs.
  1081	     2	//
  1082	     3	// Vector sources:
  1083	     4	//   codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-api-key-stamper-0.5.0/ts-source/__fixtures__/api-key.{private,public}
  1084	     5	//   codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-api-key-stamper-0.5.0/ts-source/__tests__/stamp-test.ts
  1085	     6	//
  1086	     7	// P-256 ECDSA signatures are not deterministic across libraries (noble uses
  1087	     8	// RFC 6979; BouncyCastle's HMacDsaKCalculator also uses RFC 6979 with the
  1088	     9	// same digest, so for identical input + key + nonce-source the signature
  1089	    10	// is deterministic). Tests therefore assert:
  1090	    11	//   - the header name == "X-Stamp"
  1091	    12	//   - the base64url-decoded JSON has keys ["publicKey", "scheme", "signature"]
  1092	    13	//   - publicKey matches the configured key
  1093	    14	//   - scheme == "SIGNATURE_SCHEME_TK_API_P256"
  1094	    15	//   - the signature is a valid DER-encoded ECDSA signature over SHA-256(content)
  1095	    16	//     under the configured public key
  1096	    17	
  1097	    18	using System;
  1098	    19	using System.IO;
  1099	    20	using System.Text;
  1100	    21	using System.Text.Json;
  1101	    22	using FluentAssertions;
  1102	    23	using Org.BouncyCastle.Asn1;
  1103	    24	using Org.BouncyCastle.Asn1.X9;
  1104	    25	using Org.BouncyCastle.Crypto.Parameters;
  1105	    26	using Org.BouncyCastle.Math;
  1106	    27	using Org.BouncyCastle.Security;
  1107	    28	using Xunit;
  1108	    29	
  1109	    30	namespace Turnkey.Tests
  1110	    31	{
  1111	    32	    public class ApiKeyStamperTests
  1112	    33	    {
  1113	    34	        // From upstream __fixtures__/api-key.{private,public}
  1114	    35	        private const string FixturePrivateKey =
  1115	    36	            "487f361ddfd73440e707f4daa6775b376859e8a3c9f29b3bb694a12927c0213c";
  1116	    37	        private const string FixturePublicKey =
  1117	    38	            "02f739f8c77b32f4d5f13265861febd76e7a9c61a1140d296b8c16302508870316";
  1118	    39	
  1119	    40	        [Fact]
  1120	    41	        public void Constructor_DefersValidation()
  1121	    42	        {
  1122	    43	            // Upstream `new ApiKeyStamper({apiPublicKey, apiPrivateKey})` only
  1123	    44	            // assigns fields; validation happens at sign time. C# mirrors that.
  1124	    45	            Action act = () => new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, "abcd");
  1125	    46	            act.Should().NotThrow();
  1126	    47	        }
  1127	    48	
  1128	    49	        [Fact]
  1129	    50	        public void SignWithApiKey_RejectsBadKeyLength()
  1130	    51	        {
  1131	    52	            // Validation now occurs in SignWithApiKey -> Crypto.GetPublicKey.
  1132	    53	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, "abcd");
  1133	    54	            Action act = () => stamper.SignWithApiKey("payload");
  1134	    55	            act.Should().Throw<ArgumentException>()
  1135	    56	               .WithMessage("invalid P-256 private key: expected 32 bytes, got 2");
  1136	    57	        }
  1137	    58	
  1138	    59	        [Fact]
  1139	    60	        public void SignWithApiKey_RejectsZeroScalar()
  1140	    61	        {
  1141	    62	            string zero = new string('0', 64);
  1142	    63	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, zero);
  1143	    64	            Action act = () => stamper.SignWithApiKey("payload");
  1144	    65	            act.Should().Throw<ArgumentException>()
  1145	    66	               .WithMessage("invalid P-256 private key: scalar must be in [1, n - 1]");
  1146	    67	        }
  1147	    68	
  1148	    69	        [Fact]
  1149	    70	        public void Stamp_UpstreamFixture_ProducesValidWireBytes()
  1150	    71	        {
  1151	    72	            // Matches upstream __tests__/stamp-test.ts:6-37
  1152	    73	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
  1153	    74	            const string messageToSign = "hello from TKHQ!";
  1154	    75	
  1155	    76	            var result = stamper.Stamp(messageToSign);
  1156	    77	
  1157	    78	            result.StampHeaderName.Should().Be("X-Stamp");
  1158	    79	
  1159	    80	            // Decode base64url -> JSON -> object.
  1160	    81	            string decodedJson = Encoding.DecodeBase64UrlToString(result.StampHeaderValue);
  1161	    82	
  1162	    83	            using var doc = JsonDocument.Parse(decodedJson);
  1163	    84	            var root = doc.RootElement;
  1164	    85	
  1165	    86	            // Keys must be exactly publicKey, scheme, signature in this order.
  1166	    87	            int i = 0;
  1167	    88	            string[] expectedKeys = { "publicKey", "scheme", "signature" };
  1168	    89	            foreach (var prop in root.EnumerateObject())
  1169	    90	            {
  1170	    91	                prop.Name.Should().Be(expectedKeys[i++]);
  1171	    92	            }
  1172	    93	            i.Should().Be(3);
  1173	    94	
  1174	    95	            root.GetProperty("publicKey").GetString().Should().Be(FixturePublicKey);
  1175	    96	            root.GetProperty("scheme").GetString().Should().Be("SIGNATURE_SCHEME_TK_API_P256");
  1176	    97	
  1177	    98	            // Verify the DER ECDSA signature against SHA-256(content) under the
  1178	    99	            // public key. This is the wire-format gate: if the signature isn't
  1179	   100	            // verifiable against the configured P-256 key, the stamp would be
  1180	   101	            // rejected by Turnkey's backend.
  1181	   102	            string signatureHex = root.GetProperty("signature").GetString()!;
  1182	   103	            byte[] signatureBytes = HexToBytes(signatureHex);
  1183	   104	            AssertSignatureVerifies(FixturePublicKey, messageToSign, signatureBytes);
  1184	   105	        }
  1185	   106	
  1186	   107	        [Fact]
  1187	   108	        public void SignWithApiKey_ProducesDerHexThatVerifies()
  1188	   109	        {
  1189	   110	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
  1190	   111	            const string content = "any payload we care to sign";
  1191	   112	            string sigHex = stamper.SignWithApiKey(content);
  1192	   113	
  1193	   114	            // Output is DER hex; even length, starts with 30 (SEQUENCE tag).
  1194	   115	            (sigHex.Length % 2).Should().Be(0);
  1195	   116	            sigHex.Substring(0, 2).Should().Be("30");
  1196	   117	
  1197	   118	            AssertSignatureVerifies(FixturePublicKey, content, HexToBytes(sigHex));
  1198	   119	        }
  1199	   120	
  1200	   121	        [Fact]
  1201	   122	        public void SignWithApiKey_DeterministicWithRfc6979()
  1202	   123	        {
  1203	   124	            // RFC 6979 + same key + same content + same SHA-256(content) +
  1204	   125	            // same low-S normalization => signature is bit-identical across
  1205	   126	            // invocations (HMacDsaKCalculator is deterministic).
  1206	   127	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
  1207	   128	            string a = stamper.SignWithApiKey("the same content");
  1208	   129	            string b = stamper.SignWithApiKey("the same content");
  1209	   130	            a.Should().Be(b);
  1210	   131	        }
  1211	   132	
  1212	   133	        [Fact]
  1213	   134	        public void SignWithApiKey_LowSEnforced()
  1214	   135	        {
  1215	   136	            // For RFC 6979 + low-S: the s value's high bit (top byte) must be
  1216	   137	            // strictly less than (n / 2 + 1). Verify by parsing the DER and
  1217	   138	            // checking the s integer is in the lower half of [1, n-1].
  1218	   139	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
  1219	   140	            for (int i = 0; i < 8; i++)
  1220	   141	            {
  1221	   142	                string sigHex = stamper.SignWithApiKey("content_" + i);
  1222	   143	                var (r, s) = ParseDerEcdsa(HexToBytes(sigHex));
  1223	   144	
  1224	   145	                var curve = ECNamedCurveTable.GetByName(CryptoConstants.CURVE_NAME);
  1225	   146	                var halfN = curve.N.ShiftRight(1);
  1226	   147	                s.CompareTo(halfN).Should().BeLessOrEqualTo(0,
  1227	   148	                    "low-S requires s to be in (0, n/2]");
  1228	   149	                r.SignValue.Should().BeGreaterThan(0);
  1229	   150	                s.SignValue.Should().BeGreaterThan(0);
  1230	   151	            }
  1231	   152	        }
  1232	   153	
  1233	   154	        [Fact]
  1234	   155	        public void SignWithApiKey_WrongPublicKey_Throws()
  1235	   156	        {
  1236	   157	            // Mismatched public key (any other valid P-256 compressed key).
  1237	   158	            var other = Crypto.GenerateP256KeyPair();
  1238	   159	            Action act = () =>
  1239	   160	            {
  1240	   161	                var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(other.PublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
  1241	   162	                stamper.SignWithApiKey("payload");
  1242	   163	            };
  1243	   164	            act.Should().Throw<InvalidOperationException>()
  1244	   165	               .WithMessage("Bad API key. Expected to get public key*");
  1245	   166	        }
  1246	   167	
  1247	   168	        [Fact]
  1248	   169	        public void Stamp_HeaderConstantsAndScheme()
  1249	   170	        {
  1250	   171	            ApiKeyStamper.StampHeaderName.Should().Be("X-Stamp");
  1251	   172	            ApiKeyStamper.SignatureScheme.Should().Be("SIGNATURE_SCHEME_TK_API_P256");
  1252	   173	        }
  1253	   174	
  1254	   175	        [Fact]
  1255	   176	        public void StampHeaderValue_IsAsciiUrlSafe()
  1256	   177	        {
  1257	   178	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
  1258	   179	            var result = stamper.Stamp("hello");
  1259	   180	            // base64url alphabet: A-Z a-z 0-9 - _
  1260	   181	            foreach (char c in result.StampHeaderValue)
  1261	   182	            {
  1262	   183	                bool ok = (c >= 'A' && c <= 'Z')
  1263	   184	                          || (c >= 'a' && c <= 'z')
  1264	   185	                          || (c >= '0' && c <= '9')
  1265	   186	                          || c == '-' || c == '_';
  1266	   187	                ok.Should().BeTrue($"char '{c}' is not in base64url alphabet");
  1267	   188	            }
  1268	   189	        }
  1269	   190	
  1270	   191	        // ============================================================
  1271	   192	        // Helpers
  1272	   193	        // ============================================================
  1273	   194	
  1274	   195	        private static byte[] HexToBytes(string hex) => Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(hex);
  1275	   196	
  1276	   197	        private static void AssertSignatureVerifies(string compressedPubHex, string content, byte[] derSignature)
  1277	   198	        {
  1278	   199	            byte[] payloadBytes = System.Text.Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(content);
  1279	   200	
  1280	   201	            var digest = DigestUtilities.GetDigest("SHA-256");
  1281	   202	            digest.BlockUpdate(payloadBytes, 0, payloadBytes.Length);
  1282	   203	            var hash = new byte[digest.GetDigestSize()];
  1283	   204	            digest.DoFinal(hash, 0);
  1284	   205	
  1285	   206	            var curve = ECNamedCurveTable.GetByName(CryptoConstants.CURVE_NAME);
  1286	   207	            var domainParams = new ECDomainParameters(curve.Curve, curve.G, curve.N, curve.H, curve.GetSeed());
  1287	   208	
  1288	   209	            byte[] compressedPub = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(compressedPubHex);
  1289	   210	            var point = curve.Curve.DecodePoint(compressedPub);
  1290	   211	            var publicKey = new ECPublicKeyParameters(point, domainParams);
  1291	   212	
  1292	   213	            var signer = SignerUtilities.GetSigner("NONEwithECDSA");
  1293	   214	            signer.Init(false, publicKey);
  1294	   215	            signer.BlockUpdate(hash, 0, hash.Length);
  1295	   216	            signer.VerifySignature(derSignature).Should().BeTrue();
  1296	   217	        }
  1297	   218	
  1298	   219	        private static (BigInteger R, BigInteger S) ParseDerEcdsa(byte[] der)
  1299	   220	        {
  1300	   221	            using var ms = new MemoryStream(der);
  1301	   222	            using var reader = new Asn1InputStream(ms);
  1302	   223	            var seq = (DerSequence)reader.ReadObject();
  1303	   224	            var r = ((DerInteger)seq[0]).Value;
  1304	   225	            var s = ((DerInteger)seq[1]).Value;
  1305	   226	            return (r, s);
  1306	   227	        }
  1307	   228	    }
  1308	   229	}

  1336	     1	# Test fixtures
  1337	     2	
  1338	     3	Each subdirectory groups fixtures for one production file, plus a
  1339	     4	provenance note explaining where the bytes came from.
  1340	     5	
  1341	     6	## Provenance levels
  1342	     7	
  1343	     8	- **upstream-test-vectors** — directly copied from the upstream
  1344	     9	  `__tests__/` directory inside `codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/`.
  1345	    10	- **rfc** — standardized test vectors (RFC 5869, RFC 9180, etc.).
  1346	    11	- **nist** — NIST CAVP P-256 vectors.
  1347	    12	- **turnkey-sample** — sample bundles published by Turnkey in their
  1348	    13	  developer docs (de-identified).
  1349	    14	- **node-generated** — bytes generated by running the pinned upstream Node
  1350	    15	  package inside `tests/Fixtures/Generators/`. The generator script,
  1351	    16	  Node version, and output hash are recorded so reviewers can re-run.
  1352	    17	
  1353	    18	## Layout
  1354	    19	
  1355	    20	```
  1356	    21	Fixtures/
  1357	    22	├── encoding/
  1358	    23	│   └── turnkey-encoding-vectors.json       — upstream-test-vectors
  1359	    24	├── crypto/                                  — (added with Crypto.cs port)
  1360	    25	├── api-key-stamper/                         — (added with ApiKeyStamper.cs port)
  1361	    26	└── http/                                    — (added with Http.cs port)
  1362	    27	```
  1363	    28	
  1364	    29	## Re-generating
  1365	    30	
  1366	    31	Fixtures sourced from upstream test files are stable as long as
  1367	    32	[`codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/`](../../codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/)
  1368	    33	is not bumped. If a pin is bumped, re-extract the upstream `__tests__/`
  1369	    34	into the matching fixture file and update its sha256 in
  1370	    35	`tarball-checksums.txt` next to the new tarball.
  1371

     1	import * as os from "os";
     2	import * as fs from "fs";
     3	import * as path from "path";
     4	import * as crypto from "crypto";
     5	import { promisify } from "util";
     6	import { exec } from "child_process";
     7	
     8	const execAsync = promisify(exec);
     9	
    10	export async function generateKeyPairWithOpenSsl(): Promise<{
    11	  privateKey: string;
    12	  publicKey: string;
    13	  pemPublicKey: string;
    14	}> {
    15	  const tmpFolder = await fs.promises.mkdtemp(
    16	    path.resolve(os.tmpdir(), "stamp-"),
    17	  );
    18	
    19	  async function contextualExec(command: string): Promise<string> {
    20	    return (
    21	      await execAsync(command, {
    22	        cwd: tmpFolder,
    23	        encoding: "utf-8",
    24	      })
    25	    ).stdout;
    26	  }
    27	
    28	  await contextualExec(
    29	    "openssl ecparam -genkey -name prime256v1 -noout -out private_key.pem",
    30	  );
    31	  await contextualExec(
    32	    "openssl ec -in private_key.pem -pubout -out public_key.pem",
    33	  );
    34	  const rawPrivateKey = await contextualExec(
    35	    "openssl ec -in private_key.pem -noout -text",
    36	  );
    37	  const rawPublicKey = await contextualExec(
    38	    "openssl ec -pubin -in public_key.pem -conv_form compressed -noout -text",
    39	  );
    40	
    41	  const privateKey = rawPrivateKey
    42	    .split("\n")
    43	    .slice(2, 5)
    44	    .map((line) => line.replace(/:/g, "").trim())
    45	    .join("");
    46	
    47	  const publicKey = rawPublicKey
    48	    .split("\n")
    49	    .slice(2, 5)
    50	    .map((line) => line.replace(/:/g, "").trim())
    51	    .join("");
    52	
    53	  const pemPublicKey = (
    54	    await fs.promises.readFile(
    55	      path.resolve(tmpFolder, "public_key.pem"),
    56	      "utf-8",
    57	    )
    58	  ).trim();
    59	
    60	  await fs.promises.rm(tmpFolder, { recursive: true, force: true });
    61	
    62	  return { privateKey, publicKey, pemPublicKey };
    63	}
    64	
    65	export function assertValidSignature({
    66	  content,
    67	  pemPublicKey,
    68	  signature,
    69	}: {
    70	  content: string;
    71	  pemPublicKey: string;
    72	  signature: string;
    73	}): true {
    74	  const verifier = crypto.createVerify("SHA256");
    75	  verifier.update(content);
    76	  verifier.end();
    77	
    78	  if (verifier.verify(pemPublicKey, signature, "hex")) {
    79	    return true;
    80	  }
    81	
    82	  throw new Error(
    83	    [
    84	      `Invalid signature.`,
    85	      `content: ${JSON.stringify(content)}`,
    86	      `pemPublicKey: ${JSON.stringify(pemPublicKey)}`,
    87	      `signature: ${JSON.stringify(signature)}`,
    88	    ].join("\n"),
    89	  );
    90	}

     2	import * as path from "path";
     3	
     4	const FIXTURES_DIR = path.resolve(__dirname, "..", "__fixtures__");

     6	export async function readFixture(): Promise<{
     7	  privateKey: string;
     8	  publicKey: string;
     9	  pemPublicKey: string;

    28	      "utf-8",
    29	    )
    30	  ).trim();
    31	
    32	  return {
    33	    privateKey,
    34	    publicKey,
    35	    pemPublicKey,
    36	  };
    37	}

   349	        /// <summary>

   353	        public static byte[] GetPublicKey(byte[] privateKey, bool isCompressed = true)

    91	        {

    95	            }

    97	            // Upstream uses Array.prototype.reduce starting at "". For an empty

   100	            {

codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:3740:/bin/zsh -lc "node -e \"function hexToAscii(s){let o=''; for(let i=0;i<s.length;i+=2)o+=String.fromCharCode(parseInt(s.substr(i,2),16)); return Array.from(o).map(c=>c.charCodeAt(0)).join(',');} function b64urlToBase64(input){let b64=input.replace(/-/g,'+').replace(/_/g,'/'); const padLen=(4-(b64.length%4))%4; return b64+'='.repeat(padLen);} function atob2(input){const str=String(input).replace(/["'^A-Za-z0-9+/=]/g,'"''); if(str.length%4===1) throw new Error('InvalidCharacterError'); const keyStr='ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789+/='; let output='',buffer=0,bits=0,i=0; while(i<str.length){const ch=str.charAt(i); const index=keyStr.indexOf(ch); if(index<0||index>64){i++; continue;} if(ch==='='){bits=0;} else {buffer=(buffer<<6)|index; bits+=6;} while(bits>=8){bits-=8; output+=String.fromCharCode((buffer>>bits)&0xff);} i++;} return output;} console.log('hex f ->', hexToAscii('f')); console.log('hex zz ->', hexToAscii('zz')); console.log('decode a*GVsbG8 ->', atob2(b64urlToBase64('a*GVsbG8')));\"" in /Users/takeshi/Kyuzan/src/turnkey-sdk-csharp

codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523-prefix-evidence.md:6354:./codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:3740:/bin/zsh -lc "node -e \"function hexToAscii(s){let o=''; for(let i=0;i<s.length;i+=2)o+=String.fromCharCode(parseInt(s.substr(i,2),16)); return Array.from(o).map(c=>c.charCodeAt(0)).join(',');} function b64urlToBase64(input){let b64=input.replace(/-/g,'+').replace(/_/g,'/'); const padLen=(4-(b64.length%4))%4; return b64+'='.repeat(padLen);} function atob2(input){const str=String(input).replace(/["'^A-Za-z0-9+/=]/g,'"''); if(str.length%4===1) throw new Error('InvalidCharacterError'); const keyStr='ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789+/='; let output='',buffer=0,bits=0,i=0; while(i<str.length){const ch=str.charAt(i); const index=keyStr.indexOf(ch); if(index<0||index>64){i++; continue;} if(ch==='='){bits=0;} else {buffer=(buffer<<6)|index; bits+=6;} while(bits>=8){bits-=8; output+=String.fromCharCode((buffer>>bits)&0xff);} i++;} return output;} console.log('hex f ->', hexToAscii('f')); console.log('hex zz ->', hexToAscii('zz')); console.log('decode a*GVsbG8 ->', atob2(b64urlToBase64('a*GVsbG8')));\"" in /Users/takeshi/Kyuzan/src/turnkey-sdk-csharp

codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:2694:codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:651:src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:6054:| `Stamp(string payload)` (`src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:118`) | `ApiKeyStamper.stamp(payload)` (`ts-source/index.ts:84`) | REVIEWED | JSON stamp field names/order and `X-Stamp` value construction reviewed. |
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:6055:| `SignWithApiKey(string content)` (`src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:148`) | `purejs.signWithApiKey(input)` (`ts-source/purejs.ts:5`) | REVIEWED | Mirrors purejs path: derive compressed pubkey, compare, SHA-256, P-256 ECDSA, low-S, DER hex. |
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:6056:| `EncodeDerSignatureHex(BigInteger r, BigInteger s)` (`src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:199`) | `signature.toDERHex()` (`ts-source/purejs.ts:23`) | REVIEWED | Upstream DER implementation is in `@noble/curves`, not local TS; local DER positive INTEGER encoding reviewed against expected DER form. |
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:6061:- TS `async`/dynamic import -> synchronous C# calls: no JS module runtime in C#; output is immediate equivalent for the reviewed purejs path.
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:6076:| `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:148` and comment scope at `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:13` | `ts-source/index.ts:31`, `ts-source/index.ts:56`, `ts-source/index.ts:84` | C# always follows the purejs-style deterministic RFC6979 path. Upstream `ApiKeyStamper.stamp()` defaults through `detectRuntime()`, so in Node it dispatches to `nodecrypto.ts`, whose ECDSA signature bytes are not expected to be deterministic. Repeated C# stamps can therefore differ from upstream default Node stamps even for the same key/payload. | Make the parity target explicit in tests and docs as `runtimeOverride = "purejs"` parity, or change the C# implementation/fixtures to match the selected upstream default behavior. |
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:6077:| `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:62` | `ts-source/index.ts:78`, `ts-source/purejs.ts:10` | C# validates/parses the private key in the constructor and throws before any signing call. Upstream constructor only assigns fields; invalid key material is rejected later when signing derives/imports the key. This changes error timing and observable construction behavior. | Defer private-key parsing/range validation until `SignWithApiKey`, or explicitly document constructor-time validation as an intentional behavior change. |
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:6083:- No fixture under `tests/Fixtures/` currently exercises `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs`. `tests/Fixtures/README.md` lists `api-key-stamper/`, but that directory is absent.
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:6084:- `tests/ApiKeyStamperTests.cs` uses upstream fixture constants inline, but it does not assert exact Node-generated stamp bytes. It verifies JSON key order, constants, DER shape, signature validity, determinism, and low-S behavior.
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:6090:- Exact C# signature bytes were not compared against a pinned upstream purejs-generated fixture because no such fixture exists in `tests/Fixtures/`.
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:6091:- The review assumes the intended parity target is upstream `purejs.ts`, based on the file comments; if the target is upstream package default behavior in Node, section E item 1 is blocking.

Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:6573:  6053	| `ApiKeyStamper(string apiPublicKey, string apiPrivateKey)` (`src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:62`) | `ApiKeyStamper.constructor(config)` (`ts-source/index.ts:78`) | REVIEWED | Stores key material, but C# also validates private key at construction; see E. |
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:6574:  6054	| `Stamp(string payload)` (`src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:118`) | `ApiKeyStamper.stamp(payload)` (`ts-source/index.ts:84`) | REVIEWED | JSON stamp field names/order and `X-Stamp` value construction reviewed. |
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:6575:  6055	| `SignWithApiKey(string content)` (`src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:148`) | `purejs.signWithApiKey(input)` (`ts-source/purejs.ts:5`) | REVIEWED | Mirrors purejs path: derive compressed pubkey, compare, SHA-256, P-256 ECDSA, low-S, DER hex. |
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:6576:  6056	| `EncodeDerSignatureHex(BigInteger r, BigInteger s)` (`src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:199`) | `signature.toDERHex()` (`ts-source/purejs.ts:23`) | REVIEWED | Upstream DER implementation is in `@noble/curves`, not local TS; local DER positive INTEGER encoding reviewed against expected DER form. |
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:6581:  6061	- TS `async`/dynamic import -> synchronous C# calls: no JS module runtime in C#; output is immediate equivalent for the reviewed purejs path.
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:6596:  6076	| `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:148` and comment scope at `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:13` | `ts-source/index.ts:31`, `ts-source/index.ts:56`, `ts-source/index.ts:84` | C# always follows the purejs-style deterministic RFC6979 path. Upstream `ApiKeyStamper.stamp()` defaults through `detectRuntime()`, so in Node it dispatches to `nodecrypto.ts`, whose ECDSA signature bytes are not expected to be deterministic. Repeated C# stamps can therefore differ from upstream default Node stamps even for the same key/payload. | Make the parity target explicit in tests and docs as `runtimeOverride = "purejs"` parity, or change the C# implementation/fixtures to match the selected upstream default behavior. |
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:6597:  6077	| `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:62` | `ts-source/index.ts:78`, `ts-source/purejs.ts:10` | C# validates/parses the private key in the constructor and throws before any signing call. Upstream constructor only assigns fields; invalid key material is rejected later when signing derives/imports the key. This changes error timing and observable construction behavior. | Defer private-key parsing/range validation until `SignWithApiKey`, or explicitly document constructor-time validation as an intentional behavior change. |
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:6603:  6083	- No fixture under `tests/Fixtures/` currently exercises `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs`. `tests/Fixtures/README.md` lists `api-key-stamper/`, but that directory is absent.
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:6604:  6084	- `tests/ApiKeyStamperTests.cs` uses upstream fixture constants inline, but it does not assert exact Node-generated stamp bytes. It verifies JSON key order, constants, DER shape, signature validity, determinism, and low-S behavior.
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:6610:  6090	- Exact C# signature bytes were not compared against a pinned upstream purejs-generated fixture because no such fixture exists in `tests/Fixtures/`.
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:6611:  6091	- The review assumes the intended parity target is upstream `purejs.ts`, based on the file comments; if the target is upstream package default behavior in Node, section E item 1 is blocking.

Upstream constructor only assigns fields; invalid key material is rejected later when signing derives/imports the key.
