codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:6655:  6135	- No fixture under `tests/Fixtures/` currently exercises `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs`. `tests/Fixtures/README.md` lists `api-key-stamper/`, but that directory is absent.
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:6656:  6136	- `tests/ApiKeyStamperTests.cs` uses upstream fixture constants inline, but it does not assert exact Node-generated stamp bytes. It verifies JSON key order, constants, DER shape, signature validity, determinism, and low-S behavior.
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:6662:  6142	- Exact C# signature bytes were not compared against a pinned upstream purejs-generated fixture because no such fixture exists in `tests/Fixtures/`.

codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:7985:  1321	     9	    "note": "P-256 ECDSA signatures are NOT byte-identical across runtimes when the upstream impl uses random-k (nodecrypto.ts, webcrypto.ts). This C# port mirrors upstream's purejs runtime which uses RFC 6979 deterministic-k, so the signature bytes are reproducible. Either way, all signatures verify under the same public key + SHA-256(content)."

codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:17489:./codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:3740:/bin/zsh -lc "node -e \"function hexToAscii(s){let o=''; for(let i=0;i<s.length;i+=2)o+=String.fromCharCode(parseInt(s.substr(i,2),16)); return Array.from(o).map(c=>c.charCodeAt(0)).join(',');} function b64urlToBase64(input){let b64=input.replace(/-/g,'+').replace(/_/g,'/'); const padLen=(4-(b64.length%4))%4; return b64+'='.repeat(padLen);} function atob2(input){const str=String(input).replace(/["'^A-Za-z0-9+/=]/g,'"''); if(str.length%4===1) throw new Error('InvalidCharacterError'); const keyStr='ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789+/='; let output='',buffer=0,bits=0,i=0; while(i<str.length){const ch=str.charAt(i); const index=keyStr.indexOf(ch); if(index<0||index>64){i++; continue;} if(ch==='='){bits=0;} else {buffer=(buffer<<6)|index; bits+=6;} while(bits>=8){bits-=8; output+=String.fromCharCode((buffer>>bits)&0xff);} i++;} return output;} console.log('hex f ->', hexToAscii('f')); console.log('hex zz ->', hexToAscii('zz')); console.log('decode a*GVsbG8 ->', atob2(b64urlToBase64('a*GVsbG8')));\"" in /Users/takeshi/Kyuzan/src/turnkey-sdk-csharp

codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:308:src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5571:- I could not verify Noble `@noble/curves@1.9.0` low-S verification defaults from source in this checkout. If Noble rejects high-S signatures by default while BouncyCastle accepts them, `VerifyP256RawSignature` and `VerifyEnclaveSignature` need an explicit `s <= n/2` check.
codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5658:- I could not verify Noble `@noble/curves@1.9.0` low-S verification defaults from source in this checkout. If Noble rejects high-S signatures by default while BouncyCastle accepts them, `VerifyP256RawSignature` and `VerifyEnclaveSignature` need an explicit `s <= n/2` check.

codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523-prefix-evidence.md:3753:./src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:398:src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:2025:   651	src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:2774:  1400	src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:4068:  2694	codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:651:src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:6055:  5535	./src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:7062:   398	src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:8658:tests/Fixtures/api-key-stamper/turnkey-api-key-stamper-vectors.json:9:    "note": "P-256 ECDSA signatures are NOT byte-identical across runtimes when the upstream impl uses random-k (nodecrypto.ts, webcrypto.ts). This C# port mirrors upstream's purejs runtime which uses RFC 6979 deterministic-k, so the signature bytes are reproducible. Either way, all signatures verify under the same public key + SHA-256(content)."

codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:9201:codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:2694:codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:651:src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:9864:codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:4068:  2694	codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:651:src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:651:src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:1400:src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:2694:codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:651:src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:3693:codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5571:- I could not verify Noble `@noble/curves@1.9.0` low-S verification defaults from source in this checkout. If Noble rejects high-S signatures by default while BouncyCastle accepts them, `VerifyP256RawSignature` and `VerifyEnclaveSignature` need an explicit `s <= n/2` check.
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:3705:codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5658:- I could not verify Noble `@noble/curves@1.9.0` low-S verification defaults from source in this checkout. If Noble rejects high-S signatures by default while BouncyCastle accepts them, `VerifyP256RawSignature` and `VerifyEnclaveSignature` need an explicit `s <= n/2` check.

codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:5535:./src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:14888:./src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

   118	
   119	## Review run
   120	
   121	```bash
   122	# Round N for file Crypto.cs (replace as needed)
   123	./codex-crypto-review.sh Crypto.cs 1

   127	
   128	Each invocation appends a `.md` file in this directory with the codex output
   129	unchanged. Do not edit the output after the run — it is the evidence.
   130	
   131	## When to re-run
   132	
   133	- Bump any pinned `@turnkey/*` version → re-run 3 rounds per file.
   134	- Substantive change to a `src/*.cs` file → re-run 3 rounds for that file.
   135	- Bump BouncyCastle major → re-run Crypto.cs + ApiKeyStamper.cs + Http.cs.

0c9e1ec Fix ApiKeyStamper.cs divergences flagged by Codex r1
 src/ApiKeyStamper.cs | 60 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++---------------------
 1 file changed, 36 insertions(+), 24 deletions(-)

codex
A key review nuance is showing up: the C# file now explicitly targets upstream’s `purejs` signing path, while the upstream universal `ApiKeyStamper` chooses Node crypto under Node by default. I’m checking whether the tests and fixture gate actually prove byte parity for that target, because that affects sections E and F.

**A. Source Pin Acknowledgement**
- Upstream package: `@turnkey/api-key-stamper`
- Version: `0.5.0`
- Tarball SHA-256: `962a2d22c7c40240f05be98533769b37ab7dad7dbb5abec762c41007233d02bd`
- C# file git SHA: `0c9e1ec0cadd9dfa97b45f404628a2f6755f8757`

**B. Method Coverage Table**
| C# method (file:line) | Upstream TS function (path:line) | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ApiKeyStamper(string,string)` (`src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:82`) | `ApiKeyStamper.constructor` (`ts-source/index.ts:78`) | REVIEWED | Stores key material; validation deferred for non-null valid strings. |
| `Stamp(string)` (`src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:120`) | `ApiKeyStamper.stamp` (`ts-source/index.ts:84`) | REVIEWED | Stamp object field order and `X-Stamp` shape reviewed. |
| `SignWithApiKey(string)` (`src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:150`) | `signWithApiKey` dispatch (`ts-source/index.ts:48`), `purejs.signWithApiKey` (`ts-source/purejs.ts:5`) | REVIEWED | C# follows purejs-style path, not upstream default Node dispatch. See E. |
| `EncodeDerSignatureHex(BigInteger,BigInteger)` (`src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:211`) | `signature.toDERHex()` call (`ts-source/purejs.ts:23`); DER logic comparable to `convertEcdsaIeee1363ToDer` (`ts-source/webcrypto.ts:86`) | REVIEWED | DER INTEGER padding/minimal encoding reviewed. |

**C. Intentional Adaptations**
- TS async/dynamic import -> synchronous C# method calls: structural C# API shape change only for an already-selected signing backend.
- TS config object -> C# constructor parameters: API ergonomics change; valid key strings produce the same stamp inputs.
- `JSON.stringify` object -> `System.Text.Json` source-generated DTO: field names/order are explicitly fixed as `publicKey`, `scheme`, `signature`.
- JS byte/hex helpers -> shared C# `Encoding` helpers: structural reuse of the SDK’s ported encoding primitives.
- Noble P-256 primitives -> BouncyCastle P-256 primitives: structural backend swap for EC math/signing, but see E for low-S/runtime behavior.

**D. D17 Enforcement Check**
N/A (not Crypto.cs).

**E. Logic Divergence Findings**
| C# file:line | TS upstream path:line | Semantic diff | Suggested fix |
|---|---|---|---|
| `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:13`, `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:150` | `ts-source/index.ts:31`, `ts-source/index.ts:56`, `ts-source/index.ts:84` | C# always uses the deterministic purejs-style path. Upstream `ApiKeyStamper.stamp()` with no override detects Node and dispatches to `nodecrypto.ts`, which signs via Node crypto and does not provide byte-stable signatures. | Either implement runtime selection/config parity, or formally scope this file/tests to upstream `runtimeOverride: "purejs"` and update fixture gate wording accordingly. |
| `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:196` | `ts-source/purejs.ts:22` | C# unconditionally normalizes `s` to low-S. Upstream purejs calls `p256.sign(hash, privateKey)` with no options. The package depends on `@noble/curves` v1 range (`^1.3.0`), and noble’s release notes say low-S became default for ECDSA in v2, which implies v1 P-256 default behavior is not the same low-S policy. Source: https://github.com/paulmillr/noble-curves/releases | Remove C# low-S normalization for v1 purejs parity, or generate upstream vectors with an explicit low-S policy and document that this intentionally differs from `@turnkey/api-key-stamper@0.5.0`. |
| `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:84` | `ts-source/index.ts:78` | C# constructor rejects `null`; TS constructor only assigns `config.apiPublicKey` / `config.apiPrivateKey`. Invalid-input behavior differs. | If invalid-input parity matters, defer null/key validation to sign time; otherwise document this as a C# contract difference. |
| `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:122`, `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:152` | `ts-source/index.ts:84`, `ts-source/purejs.ts:21` | C# rejects null payload/content with `ArgumentNullException`; TS has no explicit null guard and failure comes from downstream JS hashing/signing behavior. | Match upstream lazy failure behavior or document C# non-null API precondition. |
| `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs:163` | `ts-source/purejs.ts:10` | Invalid private-key errors come from C# `Crypto.GetPublicKey` custom validation/messages; upstream errors come from noble `p256.getPublicKey`. | If error-message parity is required, add tests against upstream invalid vectors and normalize C# exception messages. |

**F. Fixture Comparison Gate**
| Fixture | Pinned upstream provenance | C# byte assertion status |
|---|---|---|
| `tests/Fixtures/api-key-stamper/turnkey-api-key-stamper-vectors.json` | Provenance points to `@turnkey/api-key-stamper@0.5.0`, the correct tarball SHA, upstream `api-key.{public,private}`, and `stamp-test.ts`. | Not all fixtures match. The fixture is an upstream-test-vector shape/provenance file, not a Node-generated exact byte vector. `tests/ApiKeyStamperTests.cs` uses inline fixture constants and asserts key order, constants, DER validity, determinism, and low-S, but it does not assert exact `signature` or `stampHeaderValue` bytes produced by pinned upstream Node/purejs. |

**G. Unresolved Assumptions**
- I did not run `dotnet test`; the current sandbox is read-only and test/build commands would write artifacts.
- The upstream tarball does not include `node_modules`, so exact installed `@noble/curves` source for `@turnkey/api-key-stamper@0.5.0` was not locally executable from the snapshot.
- The fixture gate is ambiguous: default Node upstream bytes are intentionally non-deterministic, while purejs bytes are deterministic but not currently recorded/asserted.
- Round 2 does not meet the stated PASS criterion because E has entries and F is not “all fixtures match.”
